Problem Title: Build Array Where You Can Find The Maximum Exactly K Comparisons
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 3D grid. The x-axis is array length (N), y-axis is max value (M), z-axis is cost (K). Draw an arrow staying on the same z-plane (x <= j), and an arrow jumping up a z-plane (x > j).
Draw two tables side-by-side. Draw straight lines connecting matching IDs. For unconnected Queries rows, draw a dotted line dropping into a "Default 0" bucket.

Problem Title: Maximum Score After Splitting a String
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Write "Total 1s: X" above it. Draw a sliding vertical partition. Keep two small chalkboards next to it ("Left 0s" and "Right 1s") that increment/decrement instantly as the partition shifts one space.

Problem Title: Maximum Points You Can Obtain from Cards
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a long array. Draw a box of length `N-K` over the array. Slide the box from left to right. Whatever sum is left OUTSIDE the box is your score. Find the box placement that minimizes the inside sum.
Draw the jagged grid. Draw diagonal stripes across it, labeling them "Sum=0", "Sum=1", "Sum=2". Draw buckets below matching these sums. Drop numbers into their respective sum bucket.

Draw the array. Beside it, draw a horizontal pipe (the deque). As you calculate values, drop them in the right side. If a new value is bigger than older ones in the pipe, push the older ones out the back. Only the strongest/freshest survive.
Draw a list of numbers. Draw a circle representing a "Knowledge Bucket" (Set). Pour all numbers into the bucket. Then point to each number in the list and ask the bucket "Do you have my+1?".
Draw a dial with numbers 0 to Length-1. Draw arrows jumping forward and backward on the dial. Mark the final resting position. Perform one single string cut at that mark.

Problem Title: Leftmost Column with at Least a One
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Start at [0][Cols-1]. Draw a "staircase" line: left, left, down, left, down, down. The path only moves left or down, never back.

Optimised Pattern: Linked Hash Map / Queue + Hash Map
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a Queue and a Map. When a duplicate enters, mark it in the Map. When "First Unique" is called, pop the "garbage" (duplicates) off the front of the queue until a "clean" unique number is visible.

Problem Title: Check If a String Is a Valid Sequence from Root to Leaves in a Binary Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the tree and the target array. Place a finger on the tree root and a finger on the array's first index. Move both. If the node doesn't match the finger on the array, stop immediately.

Problem Title: Kids With the Greatest Number of Candies
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a horizontal line representing the 'Max Candy' level. For each kid, draw a dashed line extending their candy bar by the 'extra' amount. If it touches or crosses the Max line, mark True.

Problem Title: Max Difference You Can Get From Changing an Integer
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the digits as slots. For MAX, look left-to-right for the first "weak" slot (not 9) and fill it/all its twins with 9s. For MIN, look for the first slot to force toward 0 or 1.

Problem Title: Check If a String Can Break Another String
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two arrays of sorted characters, one above the other. Draw vertical lines connecting `s1[i]` to `s2[i]`. If all arrows point "up" or all arrows point "down", it's a break.

Problem Title: Number of Ways to Wear Different Hats to Each Other
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid: Rows = Hats (1-40), Cols = Bitmask (0-1023). For Hat 1, decide which person in the mask gets it. Move to Hat 2. Use bitwise OR to update masks.
Draw a static "Bin Template" table. Draw the Sessions table passing through a "Categorizer" funnel. Match the categorized results to the template and fill in the counts.
Draw two buckets: "Outgoing" and "Incoming". Throw the first city of every pair into Outgoing. Then look at the second city of every pair—if it can't find itself in the Outgoing bucket, it's the end.

Problem Title: Check If All 1's Are at Least Length K Places Away
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Place a marker at the first '1'. As you move to the next '1', draw a bridge between them and count the empty slots. If bridge < K, fail.

Problem Title: Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff <= Limit
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a window over an array. Below it, draw two "tubes" (deques). One holds the "kings" (descending maxes), one holds the "peasants" (ascending mins). When they clash (diff > limit), push the window left.

Problem Title: Find the Kth Smallest Sum of a Matrix With Sorted Rows
Optimised Pattern: Max Heap / Binary Search on Sums
How to Draw (Opt): Draw Row 1. Add Row 2 to it to get all sums. Draw a "K-Filter" box that only lets the smallest K sums through. Feed those into Row 3.
Draw three tables. Expressions in the middle, Variables on both sides. Link them by name. Draw a logic gate (CASE) that takes the numbers and the symbol to output 'true' or 'false'.

Problem Title: Build an Array With Stack Operations
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a stream of numbers 1, 2, 3... and the Target list. For each number, if it matches Target, draw a "Push" arrow to a bucket. If it doesn't match, draw "Push" then a "Pop" arrow out of the bucket.

Problem Title: Count Triplets That Can Form Two Arrays of Equal XOR
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line of Prefix XOR values. If two points have the same Prefix XOR value, draw a bridge between them. Every integer point inside that bridge is a valid 'j'.

Problem Title: Minimum Time to Collect All Apples in a Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Starting at the leaves, if an apple exists, "light up" the edge to the parent. Parent lights up its own parent. Finally, count all "lit" edges and multiply by 2.

Problem Title: Number of Ways of Cutting a Pizza
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a pizza. Represent a cut as a state transition from a large rectangle `(r,c,R,C)` to a smaller one. Use the DP table to store the results of these smaller rectangles.
Draw the Sales table. Group rows by date. Inside each group, flip the sign of "Oranges". Sum the numbers. One table out.
Draw the string. Place a pointer that moves index by index. Above it, draw a "Streak" box that ticks up as you hit same letters and resets to 1 when a new letter appears.
Draw a grid of `i` vs `j`. In each cell, write the GCD. Circle only the cells where GCD = 1. These circled cells form your output list.

Problem Title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. At the root, write "Max=RootVal". As you move down a branch, draw an "Envelope" being passed to the child containing the updated Max. If node >= Envelope, it's a "Good Node".

Problem Title: Form Largest Integer With Digits That Add up to Target
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of size `Target + 1`. Fill it from left to right. For each cell `i`, look back at `i - cost[digit]` and append the digit to that previous string if it makes the result "larger".

Problem Title: Number of Students Doing Homework at a Given Time
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the queryTime as a vertical line. Draw each student's homework as a horizontal bar from startTime to endTime. Count how many horizontal bars the vertical line passes through.
Draw a set of colored blocks of varying lengths. Sort them by length, but ensure blocks of the same length keep their left-to-right sequence. Re-case the new head block.

Problem Title: People Whose List of Favorite Companies Is Not a Subset of Another List
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a Master Index of company names. For each person, draw a "Punch Card" with holes at the company IDs. Stack one card over another; if all holes are covered, it's a subset.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Darts Inside of a Circular Dartboard
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two points. Draw two overlapping circles of given radius that both hit those points. The centers are the candidates. Sweep an imaginary circle around each candidate.
Draw a calendar. Circle active days for a user. Assign each day a rank (1, 2, 3). Subtract: (Jan 5 - 1 = Jan 4), (Jan 6 - 2 = Jan 4). Same result = Consecutive!

Problem Title: Check If a Word Occurs As a Prefix of Any Word in a Sentence
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sentence broken into separate boxes (words). Place the prefix under each box. Compare character by character from the start of the box only.

Problem Title: Max Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Place a window over the first K chars. Draw a "-" sign at the left edge and a "+" sign at the right edge. Move the window, updating the total count instantly.

Problem Title: Pseudo-Palindromic Paths in a Binary Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Each node has a 10-bit light switch panel. As you go down, flip the switch for that digit. At the leaf, if only 0 or 1 lights are on, it's a "pseudo-palindrome".

Problem Title: Max Dot Product of Two Subsequences
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid with `nums1` on top and `nums2` on the left. In each cell, decide: "Do I multiply these two and add previous best, or just take the best from my neighbor?".
Draw two identical point sets. Link Point A to Point B ONLY if A's ID is smaller. Filter out any horizontal or vertical links. The remaining links are diagonals of valid rectangles.

Problem Title: Make Two Arrays Equal by Reversing Sub-arrays
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two grocery bags. Take an item from Bag A and put it in a "Counter" box. Take an item from Bag B and remove it from the "Counter". If the box is empty at the end, they match.

Problem Title: Check If a String Contains All Binary Codes of Size K
Optimised Pattern: Sliding Window + Hash Set (or Rolling Hash)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the binary string. Slide a window of size K. As the window moves, draw the bits being "shifted in" and "shifted out". Drop the resulting value into a "Unique Values" bucket.
Draw a square grid. Pick a "bridge" course K. Update all cells (i, j) if there's a path through K. Show the matrix filling up with "True" values.
Draw a grid. Place two markers on the same row. Draw arrows showing them jumping to the next row simultaneously. Highlight that if they land on the same cell, cherries are only counted once.

Problem Title: Maximum Product of Two Elements in an Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Draw two podiums: "1st Place" and "2nd Place". As you scan numbers, draw them challenging the current podium holders and potentially bumping them down.

Problem Title: Maximum Area of a Piece of Cake After Horizontal and Vertical Cuts
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the cake. Highlight the largest gap between horizontal lines. Highlight the largest gap between vertical lines. The intersection of these two specific gaps is the largest piece.

Problem Title: Reorder Routes to Make All Paths Lead to the City Zero
Optimised Pattern: BFS / DFS with Edge Direction Tracking
How to Draw (Opt): Draw city 0 in the center. Draw neighbors. If an original arrow points AWAY from city 0, circle it in red (requires flip). If it points TOWARD 0, it's safe.

Problem Title: Probability of a Two Boxes Having The Same Number of Distinct Balls
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a row for each color. Use boxes for the two containers. For each color, write down the combinations formula for splitting the count. Show the "distinct color" counter ticking up.
Draw the table grouped by company. Add a temporary column "CompanyMax". Calculate the tax based on "CompanyMax" and subtract from original salary.
Draw a tree. Use a magnifying glass to look at each node. If the node has only one leg (left or right), circle the foot of that leg (the child).
Draw the array with two pointers, `p1` at 0 and `p2` at N. Draw an output array. Draw an arrow from `p1` to index 0, then `p2` to index 1, then move both pointers.

Problem Title: The k Strongest Values in an Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sorted array. Place pointers at the leftmost and rightmost elements. Compare their distances to the median (middle). Pick the winner, move that pointer inward, and repeat K times.

Optimised Pattern: Two Stacks or Doubly Linked List
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two vertical tubes. One is "Past", one is "Future". As you move back, a ball drops from Past into Future. As you visit, the Future tube is emptied into a trash can.
Draw a 3D grid. X-axis: Houses, Y-axis: Target Neighborhoods, Z-axis: Colors. For each cell, look at the previous house's Z-plane and pick the cheapest compatible color.

Problem Title: Delete N Nodes After M Nodes of a Linked List
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a linked list. Draw a solid line over M nodes, then a dashed "jumping" line over the next N nodes. Show the "stitch" where the last solid node points to the end of the dashed jump.

Problem Title: Final Prices With a Special Discount in a Shop
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Below it, draw a "Waiting Room" (Stack). If a new shopper is "cheaper" than the person in the waiting room, the person in the room gets their discount and leaves.
Draw a timeline of update stickers. Instead of applying stickers to the board, just look at the stack of stickers. Pick the one on top that covers your target (r, c).

Problem Title: Find Two Non-overlapping Sub-arrays Each with Target Sum
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Slide a window. As you find a match, record its length in a "Best-So-Far" array. Then, as you find the *next* match, look at the Best-So-Far array to its left to see the shortest partner.
Draw houses as dots on a line. Group them into K circles. Inside each circle, the mailbox is at the middle dot. DP decides where the circle boundaries go to minimize total distance.

Problem Title: Sales by Day of the Week (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with 8 columns (Category + 7 Days). As you scan the orders, drop the quantity into the specific day-column that matches the date.
Draw a row of numbers. As you move right, draw an arrow pulling the "Total-So-Far" from the left and merging it into the current box.

Problem Title: Least Number of Unique Integers after K Removals
How to Draw (Opt): Draw buckets for each unique number. Sort them from shortest to tallest. Start "deleting" buckets from the left (shortest) using your total K budget until you can't afford the next one.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Days to Make m Bouquets
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a slider labeled "Days". Pick the middle day. Check the array: "Can I make M bouquets?". If Yes, move slider Left; if No, move slider Right. Repeat.

Problem Title: Kth Ancestor of a Tree Node
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Give each node a "Jetpack" with buttons: "Jump 1", "Jump 2", "Jump 4", "Jump 8". To jump 13, press 8, then 4, then 1. Total 3 jumps instead of 13.

Problem Title: Group Sold Products By The Date (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a funnel. Dump rows into buckets by date. Inside the bucket, remove duplicate names, sort them alphabetically, and join them with a comma.

Problem Title: Clone Binary Tree With Random Pointer
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the tree. When you visit a node, "stamp" a copy and record it in a ledger. If you see a random pointer to a node you've already stamped, just check the ledger and point to that copy.
Draw a single variable "Result". Draw a loop that calculates a value and immediately merges it into "Result" using an XOR gate. No array needed.
Draw a Map. Key: "file", Value: 3. When a new "file" comes in, instantly jump to checking "file(3)". No need to check (1) or (2) again.
Draw a timeline. Mark "Rain" days and "Sun" days. When a lake rains twice, look at the "Sun" days between the two rains. Pick the earliest one to "use up" and dry that lake.

Problem Title: Find Critical and Pseudo-Critical Edges in MST
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the graph. Run Kruskal's. Now, "delete" one edge and re-run. If the bridge collapses (weight goes up), that edge was Critical. If you can force it in without breaking the bank, it's Pseudo.
Draw a node. It "shouts" to its children to clone themselves. The children send back their clones, and the parent "glues" them into its own list of children.

Problem Title: Average Salary Excluding the Minimum and Maximum Salary
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. As you scan, update three small chalkboards: "Running Total", "Current Min", "Current Max". At the end, perform one subtraction and division.
Draw a line at √n. For every factor found on the left, draw a "buddy" factor on the right side. Count up the left side, then count down the right side.

Problem Title: Longest Subarray of 1's After Deleting One Element
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sliding box. If a 0 enters the box, it's okay. If a second 0 enters, slide the left side until the first 0 is pushed out. The window "breathes" as it encounters 0s.
Draw a bitmask of 1010... representing finished courses. Identify nodes with no incoming edges from 0s. Branch into all ways to pick K of those nodes to turn them into 1s.
Draw two tables side-by-side. Connect matching IDs. Shade only the rows that satisfy the June date, Movie type, and Kids-Friendly flag. Extract the Titles.
Draw a "Memory Bucket" (Set). As you walk on the grid, drop your coordinate into the bucket. If you try to drop a coordinate that's already in the bucket, a buzzer sounds (Path Crossed).

Problem Title: Check If Array Pairs Are Divisible by k
How to Draw (Opt): Draw k buckets labeled 0 to k-1. Toss numbers into buckets based on their remainder. Check if bucket r and bucket k-r have the same number of items. They "cancel" each other out.

Problem Title: Number of Subsequences That Satisfy the Given Sum Condition
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sorted array. Place pointers at ends. If sum is okay, you can keep the left element and pick any combination of the (R-L) elements in between. Use a precomputed "Powers of 2" array.
Draw a sliding window. Inside, keep a "Leaderboard" (Deque). When a new point arrives, it calculates its (y-x) and replaces anyone on the board who is "weaker" and "older".
Draw a "ID Recycle Bin" (Heap). If someone leaves, their ID goes in. If someone joins, pull from the bin first. Draw a "Chunk Directory" (Map) that points directly to owners of a chunk.

Problem Title: Countries You Can Safely Invest In (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw data pouring into Country Buckets. Calculate bucket avg. Draw a horizontal "Global Avg Line"; keep buckets above it.

Problem Title: Can Make Arithmetic Progression From Sequence
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a straight horizontal line (number line). Mark the min and max dots. Draw evenly spaced arcs between them representing the calculated 'step'. Tick off nodes as you find them.

Problem Title: Last Moment Before All Ants Fall Out of a Plank
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the plank as a straight line. Draw a left-facing arrow from the rightmost 'left' ant to index 0. Draw a right-facing arrow from the leftmost 'right' ant to index n. The answer is simply the longest arrow.

Draw a 2D grid. For row `i`, draw vertical bars going up representing the number of 1s above it. Then sweep left-to-right, drawing expanding horizontal rectangles clamped by the shortest vertical bar.

Problem Title: Minimum Possible Integer After at Most K Adjacent Swaps
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Draw a bracket of size K. Find the min digit inside. Draw a curved arrow pulling it to the front. Mark a '+1 shift' counter on the elements it jumped over.
Draw an accumulator variable `val = 0`. As you touch a parent, write `+parent`. For its children, write `-child`. Watch all pairs cancel out until only the root is left.
Draw the split array `["20th", "Oct", "2052"]`. Draw an arrow from "Oct" to a mini 2-column lookup table yielding "10". Reassemble the pieces.

Problem Title: Range Sum of Sorted Subarray Sums
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a triangle (Heap). Pop the top node, draw an arrow adding it to the result. Draw another arrow fetching the next element from the array, adding it to the popped sum, and pushing it back into the triangle.

Problem Title: Minimum Difference Between Largest and Smallest Value in Three Moves
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the sorted array. Draw a bracket covering `N-3` elements. Slide this bracket exactly 4 times (touching left edge, moving right by 1, 2, and 3 steps). Note the difference between the ends of the bracket each time.
Draw a 1D array of boxes. To fill box 10, draw backward arrows jumping to box 9 (-1), box 6 (-4), and box 1 (-9). If any target box has 'F', write 'T' in box 10.

Draw 3 separate boxes (Tables). Draw connecting lines (JOINs) between matching IDs. Draw a funnel shape. Push rows through it. At the bottom, draw a scale checking if the June pile and July pile both weigh > 100.
Draw labelled buckets. As you read the array, drop tally marks into the matching bucket. Once finished, write the `n(n-1)/2` formula under each bucket and sum them up.
Write the binary string. Draw brackets around groups of contiguous 1s. Write the length of each bracket. Plug each length into the formula and sum the results.

Draw a graph with edge decimals. Draw a starting circle. Draw expanding concentric ripples. When a ripple hits a node, update a small "score box" next to the node if the multiplied path score is higher.

Problem Title: Best Position for a Service Centre
How to Draw (Opt): Draw scattered points on a 2D plane. Drop a pin in the middle. Draw arrows stepping outward in 4 directions. Move the pin to the best option. Draw progressively shorter arrows as the pin hones in on the center of mass.
Draw the tree. Highlight P and Q. Draw a dotted line "up" from Q to see if it hits P. If it does, draw the complex "swap" arrows. Otherwise, simply cut P's top string and tie it under Q.

Problem Title: Find Users With Valid E-Mails (Database / String)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 3 large circles (States): "Start", "Prefix", and "Domain". Draw arrows between them. Start -> Prefix (requires letter). Prefix -> Prefix (allows letters/numbers/dash). Prefix -> Domain (requires @leetcode.com).
Draw N bottles. Group them visually into blocks of `(E - 1)`. The number of these blocks immediately tells you how many free refills you get.

Problem Title: Number of Nodes in the Sub-Tree With the Same Label
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. At the bottom leaves, draw small boxes like `[a:1]`. Draw arrows pushing these boxes UP to the parent. The parent combines them into a bigger box `[a:2, b:1]`, answers its own question, and pushes it up again.

Draw a string. Draw a bracket under all the 'a's, and a bracket under all the 'b's. If the brackets overlap, draw a giant bracket merging them. Finally, sort the brackets by their right edge and circle them left-to-right.

Problem Title: Find a Value of a Mysterious Function Closest to Target
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Below the current element, draw a small box (the Set) containing a few numbers. Draw an arrow pulling the *next* array element into the box, applying the `&` operator to everything inside, dropping duplicates, and updating the global minimum difference.
Draw a node with 4 children. The children pass up the numbers `[1, 4, 2, 3]`. The parent circles `4` and `3`, adds them to check the global max (7), and then passes `4 + 1 = 5` up the chain.

Problem Title: Count Odd Numbers in an Interval Range
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a block representing the range. Split the block perfectly in half visually. Check the edges of the block; if an edge lands on an odd number, add a single dot to the final sum.

Problem Title: Number of Sub-arrays With Odd Sum
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Below it, draw two buckets labeled "Even Prefixes" and "Odd Prefixes" (Even starts at 1). As you scan the array, add the current number to your total. If the total is odd, pour the "Even" bucket into your answer, then add a tally to the "Odd" bucket.

Problem Title: Number of Good Ways to Split a String
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a string. Draw a row of boxes below it (the Suffix array) filled back-to-front with unique counts. Draw a scanner moving left-to-right, tallying unique letters. At each step, compare the scanner's tally with the box immediately to its right.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Increments on Subarrays to Form a Target Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a bar chart of the array. Start from the left. Draw a horizontal line extending rightwards from the top of the first bar. When a taller bar pierces the line, color the exposed top part. The sum of these colored top parts is the answer.

Draw a database table pouring rows into a Y-shaped funnel. One branch checks if the string explicitly starts with 'DIAB1'. The other checks if ' DIAB1' is buried inside. If either is true, the row drops into the result bucket.

Draw the original string and the `indices` array on top of each other. Below them, draw a blank array of boxes. Draw a straight, single-shot arrow from `s[i]` directly into the blank box labeled `indices[i]`.
Draw the target string. Hold a token labeled '0'. Slide along the string. When the token doesn't match the current character, add a tally mark, flip the token over to '1', and keep sliding.

Problem Title: Number of Good Leaf Nodes Pairs
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a small subtree. The left child passes up `[1]`. The right passes up `[1, 2]`. The parent draws connecting lines between the left list and right list, checking sums. Then it adds 1 to all numbers, creating `[2, 2, 3]`, and pushes that box up.
Draw a 3D grid where axes are `Index`, `K`, and `LastChar`. As you move through the string, draw an arrow jumping between cells in the grid, only computing a cell if it's empty (memoization).

Draw a table of orders. Draw horizontal dividers grouping orders by `customer_id`. Inside each group, draw an arrow sorting the dates and writing "1", "2", "3" next to them. Circle the rows with numbers 1-3.

Problem Title: Find the Index of the Large Integer (Interactive)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a large block. Split it in half. Draw two hands lifting each half. An arrow points to the heavier side, and the other half is crossed out. Repeat until one small block remains.
Draw the three loops. Draw a "Gate" between the 2nd and 3rd loop. If the first condition fails, the gate slams shut, preventing the inner-most loop from running.

Problem Title: Find the Winner of an Array Game
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line of people. The first person stands on a pedestal. They fight the next person in line. If they win, draw a tally mark on their chest. If they lose, they step down and the winner takes the pedestal.

Problem Title: Minimum Swaps to Arrange a Binary Grid
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. To the right of each row, write the count of its trailing zeros (e.g., [2, 0, 1]). For row 0, you need 2 zeros. Locate the row with '2', draw a curved arrow hopping it over the other rows to the top. Count the hops.

Draw two parallel horizontal paths. Draw "sum buckets" for each path. When the paths meet at a common number, compare the buckets, empty the lighter one into the trash, and put the heavier one's total into a "Global Max" box.

Problem Title: Guess the Majority Element in a Hidden Array (Interactive)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line of mystery boxes. Query the first 4. Then drop the first and add the 5th. If the result is the same, box 1 and box 5 are identical. Use this to mark boxes with 'A' or 'B'. Count which letter appears more.
Draw the array. Below each element, write the result of `arr[i] - (i + 1)`. Use binary search pointers (`L` and `R`) to find the "jump" point where the missing count exceeds K.

Problem Title: Can Convert String in K Moves
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with 26 slots (0-25). As you calculate a shift, put a tally mark in the slot. If slot 5 has 3 tallies, the moves used are 5, 31, and 57. Check if 57 <= K.

Problem Title: Minimum Insertions to Balance a Parentheses String
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Below it, draw a "Debt Box" labeled `needed_closers`. When you see `(`, put two +1 tokens in the box. When you see `))`, take two tokens out. If the box is empty and you see `)`, add a +1 to your "Fine" (result) and take a token anyway.

Draw a 10-slot light switch board. As you read a digit '3', flip switch #3. Check a table to see if this exact switch configuration was seen before. Then flip each switch one by one to see if *that* configuration was seen (this handles the "one odd digit" case).
Draw a table with messy data ("  AppLe ", "2026-03-15"). Draw it passing through a "Squeegee" (TRIM) and a "Filter" (LOWER). The output is a clean "apple". Group these clean outputs.
Draw a vertical tube (the stack). Drop 'a' in. Next is 'A'. Since they "react," they both vanish. Drop 'b' in. Next is 'c'. They stay. The contents of the tube at the end is the "Great" string.

Problem Title: Find Kth Bit in Nth Binary String
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a long bar. Mark the center point. If K is on the right side, "fold" the bar in half. Note that you now need to "invert" the answer. Keep folding until the bar is length 1.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Non-Overlapping Subarrays With Sum Equals Target
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Draw a scanner moving right. Below it, draw a "Memory Bin" (Set). When the scanner finds a match, draw a vertical "Wall" (reset). The number of walls you draw is the answer.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost to Cut a Stick
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a stick. Pick a cut point in the middle. The stick "snaps" into two smaller sub-problems. Draw arrows pointing to these two new sticks. Use a 2D table to store the cost of every possible `(left_cut, right_cut)` pair.

Problem Title: The Most Similar Path in a Graph (Premium)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the target path as a sequence of stages. For each stage, draw all graph nodes. Draw arrows from nodes in stage 1 to their neighbors in stage 2. Each arrow carries a "cost" (0 if name matches, 1 if not).

Problem Title: The Most Recent Orders for Each Product (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table sorted by Product and Date. Draw brackets around each product. Within each bracket, write "1" next to the latest date(s), "2" next to the second latest, etc.
Draw the array. Below it, write a 0. Every time you see an odd number, erase and write a +1. If you see an even number, slam it back to 0. If it hits 3, you win.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make Array Equal
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a staircase of blocks. Draw a horizontal line cutting through the middle of the staircase. The "extra" blocks above the line perfectly fill the "gaps" below the line.
Draw a sorted number line of positions. Draw a ruler of length `d`. Place the first ball at index 0. Slide the ruler: place the next ball only where the ruler ends or further. Count balls.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Days to Eat N Oranges
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a node N. Draw only TWO branches that "jump" to `n/2` and `n/3`. Write the cost of the "jump" (1 + remainder) on the edge. This forces the tree to shrink logarithmically.
Draw a string "abc". Draw three "ghost" versions: "*bc", "a*c", "ab*". Calculate a numeric ID for each ghost and drop it into a bucket. If a bucket already has an ID, you found the pair.
Draw two tables. Draw lines connecting account IDs. Group the connected rows into "buckets" by name. Draw a scale under each bucket; only keep those weighing over 10,000.
Write the number: "1234567". Start at '7'. Draw a tally: 1, 2, 3... Dot! 1, 2, 3... Dot! Draw the result building up from right to left in a single pass.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Vertices to Reach All Nodes
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the nodes. For every edge `u -> v`, draw a red "X" on node `v` (the target). After processing all edges, circle the nodes that don't have an "X". These are your sources.

Problem Title: Minimum Numbers of Function Calls to Make Target Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the largest number in binary. Count the '1's (increments). Count the total number of bits minus 1 (doubles). Do this for all numbers, but only count the "doubles" once for the whole array.
Draw the grid. Draw a line moving through cells of 'A'. Mark each cell with a 'V'. If your next step hits a 'V' that isn't the cell you just left, draw a circle around the connection.

Problem Title: Most Visited Sector in a Circular Track
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a circle with 1 at the top. Mark the 'Start' and 'End' points. Color the path from Start to End clockwise. Those colored numbers are the sectors that were visited one extra time.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Coins You Can Get
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sorted line of numbers. Mark 'A' at the rightmost, 'Me' next to it, and 'B' at the leftmost. Move the 'A/Me' pair inward and the 'B' pointer rightward. Circle the 'Me' values.

Problem Title: Find Latest Group of Size M
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of 0s. When you put a '1' at index i, look at i-1 and i+1. If they are part of groups of size L and R, draw a single bridge connecting them. Update only the leftmost and rightmost index with L+R+1.

Draw a 2D N x N grid. To fill a cell (i, j), draw a vertical line representing a scan through all possible split points k. Use a prefix sum array on the side to quickly "weigh" the left and right halves.

Problem Title: Put Boxes Into the Warehouse I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the warehouse as a jagged ceiling. Draw a "steamroller" smoothing it out from left to right (non-increasing). Then, match the smallest boxes into the furthest available normalized slots.

Problem Title: Unique Orders and Customers Per Month (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table. Circle the rows where price > 20. Group the circled rows into boxes by month. Inside each box, count the items and count the *unique* names.

Problem Title: Detect Pattern of Length M Repeated K or More Times
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Draw two pointers: i and i+m. Slide them together. Every time they match, draw a tick mark. If you get enough ticks in a row, you've found the repeat.

Problem Title: Maximum Length of Subarray With Positive Product
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Below it, draw two rows labeled '+' and '-'. When you hit a positive, increment the '+' row. When you hit a negative, cross-swap the values from the previous step and add 1.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Days to Disconnect Island
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Run a BFS to count islands. Then, circle every '1' and pretend it's a '0' one by one, re-running BFS. If any single "circle" splits the island, answer is 1.

Problem Title: Number of Ways to Reorder Array to Get Same BST
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the root. Draw two arrows to "Left" and "Right" buckets. Write the size of each bucket. Use the formula: binom{L+R}{L} to show how many ways the two sequences can interleave.

Problem Title: Dot Product of Two Sparse Vectors
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two short lists: `[(2, 5), (10, 8)]` and `[(2, 3), (12, 1)]`. Align them vertically. Only draw a circle around the matching index 2. Ignore the rest.
Draw the tables joined. For each warehouse, draw a single "Volume Bucket." Pour all the calculated product volumes into it. The final level in the bucket is the result.
Draw the square. Draw a giant "X" across it. Number the "X" nodes from 1 to N. Note how each "i" maps to exactly two spots on the "X".

Problem Title: Number of Ways to Split a String
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 3 groups of '1's. Circle the clusters of '0's between group 1 and 2, and between 2 and 3. Write "Choices" under each circle. Multiply the choice counts.

Problem Title: Shortest Subarray to be Removed to Make Array Sorted
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two sorted segments at the edges. Draw a "gap" in the middle. Move a left finger across the prefix and a right finger across the suffix. Draw a dotted line when `left_val <= right_val`. Record the minimum gap length.
Draw a grid where rows are Cities and columns are Fuel levels. To fill a cell (C, F), draw arrows from all other cities C' where F + dist(C, C') was the previous state.

Problem Title: Replace All ?'s to Avoid Consecutive Repeating Characters
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. When you reach '?', draw two small arrows to the left and right characters. Cross out the letters those neighbors use. Write the first available letter in the box.

Problem Title: Number of Ways Where Square of One Number Equals Product of Two Numbers
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two frequency tables. Draw a square (e.g., 10^2 = 100). Now look at the other table. Find pairs like (2, 50) or (4, 25). Multiply their frequencies and add to total.

Problem Title: Minimum Time to Make Rope Colorful
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a row of same-colored balloons. Write their costs underneath. Circle the highest cost. Cross out all other costs in that group and add them to a "Total Cost" box.

Problem Title: Remove Max Number of Edges to Keep Graph Fully Traversable
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two blank graphs (Alice's and Bob's). Pick Type 3 edges first. Draw them on both graphs. If they merge components, count as "Used". Then draw Alice's specific edges only on her graph and Bob's only on his.

Problem Title: Put Boxes Into the Warehouse II (Premium)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the warehouse. From the left, draw a "Low-Ceiling" line. From the right, draw another. The effective height of a room is the *better* of the two. Match the smallest boxes to the tightest effective spots.

Problem Title: Customer Who Visited but Did Not Make Any Transactions (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two overlapping circles (Visits and Transactions). Shade the area in the Visit circle that *doesn't* overlap with Transactions. Group the customer IDs in that shaded area and count them.

Problem Title: Special Positions in a Binary Matrix
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. To the right of each row, write the row sum. Below each column, write the column sum. Circle the '1's where the corresponding row and column sums are both exactly 1.
Draw the pairs. For person x, list everyone they like more than their current partner. For each person u in that list, look up their partner v. Check if rank[u][x] < rank[u][v]. If so, x is unhappy.

Problem Title: Min Cost to Connect All Points
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the points. Circle the first point. Find the shortest line from that circle to any outside point. Draw the line and extend the circle. Repeat until all points are inside one giant circle.

Problem Title: Check If String Is Transformable With Substring Sort Operations
How to Draw (Opt): Write both strings. Use 10 queues to store the indices of each digit 0-9 in the original. For each character in the target, say '3', check the queues of '0', '1', and '2'. If any of their indices are smaller than the current '3' index, '3' is blocked and cannot move left.

Draw a BST. Draw a vertical Stack box on the side. Draw a horizontal "Cache List" below. When `next()` is called, show nodes moving from Tree arrow Stack arrow List. For `prev()`, just move a pointer i back on the List.
Draw two tables. Draw a "Join Pipe" that merges them. At the end of the pipe, draw several "Buckets" (Accounts). As data flows, it drops into the matching bucket and increments the total.

Problem Title: Sum of All Odd Length Subarrays
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Below each index, write a multiplier (e.g., i=0 is in 3 subarrays). Draw arrows from the element to all the hypothetical brackets it belongs to.

Problem Title: Maximum Sum Obtained of Any Permutation
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line of indices. For each range [start, end], write +1 at start and -1 at end+1. Sweep across to get frequencies. Draw a bar chart of frequencies and a bar chart of sorted array values. Match the tallest bars.

Draw the array and a Hash Map on the side. As you scan, write the current Sum pmod P in the Map. Draw an arrow from the current index i to a previous index j stored in the Map that satisfies the modular condition.

Draw a node for each color. Find the min/max rows and columns for Color 1. Scan that rectangle. If you see Color 2, draw an arrow 1 arrow 2. Once all arrows are drawn, try to find a "Sink" node (no outgoing arrows).
Write words as blocks. Count total empty spaces. Draw "bins" between words. Distribute spaces like cards: 1 for you, 1 for you... The "leftovers" go to a separate bin at the end.

Problem Title: Split a String Into the Max Number of Unique Substrings
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Use a vertical bar to represent a split. Move the bar one letter at a time. If the left side is "New", add to a "Seen" list and move to the right side. If you hit a duplicate, backtrack the bar.

Problem Title: Maximum Non Negative Product in a Matrix
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. In each cell, draw two boxes (Min/Max). Calculate the Max by looking at (Previous_Max · current) and (Previous_Min · current) from the top and left. The negative numbers act as "switches."

Problem Title: Minimum Cost to Connect Two Groups of Points
How to Draw (Opt): Draw Group 1 on the left and Group 2 on the right. Group 2 nodes are "lights" that can be ON or OFF. For the 1st node of Group 1, try connecting it to every light. Write down the cost and the configuration of ON lights. Move to node 2.

Problem Title: Most Frequently Ordered Products for Each Customer (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with columns: Customer, Product, Count. Draw horizontal dividers between customers. In each section, write "Rank 1" next to the highest count(s). Circle the Rank 1 rows.

Problem Title: Build Binary Expression Tree From Infix Expression
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two vertical boxes: "Values" and "Ops". Walk through the string. Push numbers. When an op has lower precedence than the stack top, pop Op, pop two Values, draw a mini-tree, and push the root back to Values.
Draw a vertical line (y-axis). A pointer starts at 0. For each log, move the pointer up or down. The final y-value is the answer.

Problem Title: Maximum Profit of Operating a Centennial Wheel
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 4-slot Gondola. Draw an "Incoming" arrow from the customers array and a "Profit" counter. Fill the gondola, update profit, and repeat until both the array and the waitlist are empty.
Draw a standard tree. Put a red "X" over nodes of people who died. Trace a line starting from the Root, following the left-most branch first. Only write down names that don't have an "X".

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Achievable Transfer Requests
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of N buildings. Underneath, write a bitmask (e.g., 101). Draw arrows between array indices based on 1s.

Problem Title: Find Nearest Right Node in Binary Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an open-ended tube (queue). Write node values passing through left-to-right, marking the target node with a star.
Draw an array of 3 boxes labeled [Big, Med, Small]. Cross out a number and write the lower number inside.

Problem Title: Alert Using Same Key-Card Three or More Times...
Optimised Pattern: Hash Map of Lists + Sorting + Sliding Window (Size 3)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a horizontal number line of minutes. Draw a bracket spanning exactly 3 points. If distance <= 60, alert.

Problem Title: Find Valid Matrix Given Row and Column Sums
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid. Put remaining row sums on the right, col sums on the bottom. Fill cell, cross out sums, write new remaining sums.

Problem Title: Find Servers That Handled Most Number of Requests
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two pyramids (heaps): "Free" and "Busy". Draw arrows moving numbers between them as time progresses.
Draw two overlapping circles (Sellers, 2020 Sales). Shade ONLY the crescent moon on the Sellers side.
Draw a bar chart of frequencies. Draw a cumulative wave moving from right to left, stopping when wave height equals the index.
Draw a tree and slice it with horizontal dashed lines. Draw a left-to-right arrow on each level validating the > or < signs.
Draw a circle with dots. Draw a pizza slice (wedge) representing the viewing angle. Rotate the slice around the circle, counting dots inside.

Problem Title: Minimum One Bit Operations to Make Integers Zero
How to Draw (Opt): Write the binary number. Cross out the leftmost 1, write its Gray Code integer equivalent underneath, and subtract the next 1's value.

Problem Title: Check If Two Expression Trees are Equivalent
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of 26 slots. Draw +1s dropping in from one tree, and -1s dropping in from the second tree to cancel them out.
Draw a factory belt generating numbers 1, 2, 3... and a trapdoor that drops numbers if they match the Customer table.

Problem Title: Maximum Nesting Depth of the Parentheses
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 2D graph. X-axis is the string index. Y-axis is depth. Draw a line going up on '(' and down on ')'. Peak is the answer.
Draw nodes sized by their degree (number of roads). Pick the two fattest nodes, sum their sizes, cross out the line between them if it exists.

Problem Title: Split Two Strings to Make Palindrome
How to Draw (Opt): Write string A on top of B. Draw an arrow pointing right from A[0] and left from B[N-1]. Connect matching outer letters with lines, leaving an isolated box in the center.

Problem Title: Count Subtrees With Max Distance Between Cities
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a binary number representing included cities. Beside it, draw a distance matrix grid. Circle the matrix intersections that correspond to the 1s in the binary number.

Problem Title: Maximum Font to Fit a Sentence in a Screen
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of fonts. Put brackets at the ends. Point an arrow at the middle. Cross out the left half if it fits, cross out the right half if it doesn't.

Problem Title: Mean of Array After Removing Some Elements
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an unsorted array. Draw two bold vertical lines at the 5% and 95% marks. Write arbitrary small numbers left of the first line, big numbers right of the second. Sum the middle.
Draw a 2D coordinate plane. Place 3 dots (towers). Pick a random coordinate square. Draw expanding rings from the towers to see if they reach that square. Tally the score.

Problem Title: Number of Sets of K Non-Overlapping Line Segments
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 2D grid for N and K. Divide each cell into two triangles (segment started vs. not started). Fill cells sequentially.
Draw an array of raw values. Draw a "lens" or "filter" box above it containing the global multiplier and adder.

Problem Title: All Valid Triplets That Can Represent a Country (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a large funnel. Pour Table A and B in, filter out duplicates. Pour the trickle into a second funnel with Table C.

Problem Title: Largest Substring Between Two Equal Characters
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of 26 empty slots. Point an anchor line from a slot to a letter. Later in the string, draw a measuring tape back to that anchor.

Problem Title: Lexicographically Smallest String After Applying Operations
Optimised Pattern: BFS / DFS with Visited State Set
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a starting node (string). Draw two branching arrows ("Add" and "Rotate"). If a branch leads to a string already drawn, draw a big red X.
Draw an array of players sorted by age. Draw curved arrows pointing from left-side players to right-side players, carrying a cumulative score sum.
Draw numbers 1 to N. Circle a number (e.g., 3). Draw lines grouping 3, 6, 9 together. When queried, just check if two numbers are in the same drawn blob.

Problem Title: Design an Expression Tree With Evaluate Function
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree where leaves are numbers and internal nodes are operators (+, -, *, /). Draw arrows bubbling values from the bottom up to the root.
Draw an array of times. Slide a 2-element window across it. Subtract the left from the right. If it beats the current record holder, overwrite the record box.
Draw a sliced array. Pluck out the smallest and largest numbers. Calculate the required "step". Toss all sliced numbers into a bucket (Set). Check if the bucket has every required "step" number.
Draw a grid. Mark each cell with its "current min-effort". Use a circle to represent the expanding frontier of the Min-Heap.
Draw groups of cells as blobs. Draw arrows from "lower value" blobs to "higher value" blobs. Label blobs with an indegree count.

Problem Title: Percentage of Users Attended a Contest (SQL)
Optimised Pattern: Group By + Scalar Subquery / Cross Join
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table split into contest-ID buckets. Draw one big box representing the Total User Count. Draw arrows from buckets to the big box.

Problem Title: Add Two Polynomials Represented as Linked Lists
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two parallel chains. Move two arrows left-to-right. Draw a new chain forming underneath as the arrows move.

Optimised Pattern: Recursive CTE (Month Generator) + LEFT JOIN + Window Functions
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a vertical timeline 1-12. Layer Driver data over it (cumulative) and Ride data (individual month snapshots).
Draw a 2-column table (Val | Freq). Below it, draw the array and use "Weight" lines (thicker lines for higher freq) to visualize the new order.

Problem Title: Widest Vertical Area Between Two Points...
How to Draw (Opt): Draw points on a graph. Draw vertical dashed lines through each point. The widest "hallway" between any two adjacent lines is the answer.

Problem Title: Count Substrings That Differ by One Character
How to Draw (Opt): Draw S horizontally and T vertically. Draw diagonal arrows. Mark a '1' when you hit a mismatch and keep counting until you hit a '2'.

Problem Title: Number of Ways to Form a Target String...
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid of target characters (rows) and dictionary columns (cols). Fill cells using: (Ways using current col * freq) + (Ways using previous col).
Draw `arr` as a long line. Draw pieces as small segments. Use arrows to "snap" piece segments onto the main line based on the starting number.
Draw 5 buckets (a, e, i, o, u). Draw n stars being distributed into these buckets. Use the formula binom{n+4}{4}.
Draw building heights as bars. Mark jumps with arrows. Use a "Top-K" box (Min-Heap) to store the L largest jumps where you used ladders.
Draw a path grid. At each junction, write the number of ways to reach the destination using combinations formula nCr. Compare K to this number.

Problem Title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Color nodes p and q red. Draw arrows "reporting" the find upwards. LCA is where the two red reports first meet.

Optimised Pattern: Recursive CTE + LEFT JOIN + SUM/COUNT Window Functions
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 12-row table. Column 1: Months. Column 2: Total Drivers (running sum). Column 3: Drivers with Rides. Column 4: Percentage calculation.
Draw an array of size n+1. Write the indices above the boxes. Fill them one by one from left to right, circling the largest number found.

Problem Title: Minimum Deletions to Make Character Frequencies Unique
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a bar chart of frequencies. If two bars are the same height, "shave" the second bar down until it is shorter than any existing unique height.

Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on Value Threshold / Greedy Math
How to Draw (Opt): Draw inventory as a histogram (columns of balls). Draw a horizontal line at height P. Calculate the area of the bars above the line using the sum of AP formula.
Draw a Binary Indexed Tree (a tree where each node covers a range of frequencies). Mark nodes that update when a new number "drops" into the tree.

Problem Title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree III
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two paths of different lengths. Show the pointers "switching" tracks to equalize the travel distance, meeting exactly at the junction.
Draw a table with 12 rows. Draw a physical bracket covering rows 1, 2, and 3. Slide it down row by row to show the average calculation.
Draw the array as a circle. Draw a highlighter "window" of size k. Rotate the highlighter while updating the running sum.

Problem Title: Minimum Deletions to Make String Balanced
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a string. As you move right, keep a tally of 'b's. When an 'a' appears, decide: "Keep a and pay in B's" or "Drop a". Write the cost underneath each char.
Draw a number line. Mark forbidden spots with 'X'. Draw circles around reachable points, expanding outwards like a ripple until you hit 'home'.
Draw a binary bitmask (e.g., 1011). Underneath, draw frequency "buckets". Try to fit the requirements of the 1-bits into the buckets.
Draw an array. Place a marker (ptr) at index 1. When a value arrives at 1, move ptr right until it hits a blank. Those skipped indices are your output.

Problem Title: Determine if Two Strings Are Close
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two bar charts (histograms) of character frequencies. Sort the bars by height. If the shapes of the two bar charts are identical, they are close.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Reduce X to Zero
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Draw a rectangular "frame" (window) sliding across it. Keep track of the frame's sum and its max width.
Draw a grid. Shade the current cell and the N cells preceding it (the "frontier"). Write the state as a base-3 number (e.g., 012).
Draw the tree levels. Draw an arrow on each level going Right-to-Left. If an arrow "jumps" to a node you've already seen on that same level, cut that branch.

Problem Title: Average Time of Process per Machine (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table where each machine has two values: a negative 'start' and a positive 'end'. Sum them and divide by count.

Problem Title: Check If Two String Arrays are Equivalent
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two rows of boxes. Place two "needles" (pointers) at the start. Move the needles char-by-char. If a needle reaches the end of a box, jump it to the next box.

Problem Title: Smallest String With A Given Numeric Value
How to Draw (Opt): Draw N empty slots. Fill them all with 'a'. Go to the last slot and turn it into 'z'. If you still have value, move left.

Problem Title: Ways to Make a Fair Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two rows: "Prefix Evens" and "Prefix Odds". When you "remove" a node, swap the "suffixes" of the two rows to see if they balance.

Problem Title: Minimum Initial Energy to Finish Tasks
How to Draw (Opt): Draw tasks as boxes with two numbers. Subtract the small number from the big one. Sort the tasks so the biggest gap comes first.

Problem Title: Change the Root of a Binary Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Pick a leaf. Draw a thick "path" to the root. Erase the parent-child arrows on that path and draw them in reverse.
Draw a single name string. Place a "Magnifier" over the first letter (UPPER) and a "Filter" over the rest (LOWER).
Draw the sequence. Below it, draw an array. When a word matches, draw a "link" to the next possible match point and add 1.
Draw list1. Draw a "cut" before a and after b. Draw list2 dropping into the gap. Draw two "welding" lines (links) connecting the pieces.
Draw two horizontal tubes meeting in the center. Mark the center point as the "Middle". Elements flow in/out of the ends or the center junction.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Removals to Make Mountain Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Below it, draw two rows of numbers. One shows "longest climb" to that point, the other "longest descent" from that point. Look for the highest sum.
Draw a table. Add a "Total" column on the right. Write the sum for each row and circle the biggest one.

Draw a vertical bucket (Stack). As you process numbers, if a "smaller" number arrives, erase the "bigger" ones above it to make room, but only if the remaining numbers in the array are enough to reach K.

Problem Title: Minimum Moves to Make Array Complementary
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a long number line (2 to 2 x limit). For each pair, draw "brackets" showing where moves increase or decrease. Sum the overlaps.
Draw a Max-Heap (Pyramid). Draw a "Min" box on the side. Pop the top of the pyramid, half it, and put it back in. Watch the "Max" and "Min" values get closer together.

Problem Title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree IV
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Mark target nodes with stars. Draw arrows moving up from stars. The highest node where the number of "star-reports" equals the target list size is the answer.
Draw a table on the left (Products) and right (Invoices). Draw lines connecting them. Collapse all invoices for one product into a single "Summary Row".
Draw a string of characters. Place a finger (pointer) on a letter. Look at the next 1-2 letters. Write the decoded result in a box underneath.
Draw a sorted line of numbers. Draw two arrows at the ends pointing inward. When they point to a pair that sums to k, circle them and move the arrows.
Write current total in binary. To add the next number, "push" the total to the left to make empty spaces, then write the next number in those spaces.
Draw a bitmask (e.g., 1011). Show it splitting into a valid submask (1001) and a remaining mask (0010). Write incompatibility sums in the nodes.
Draw an N x N grid. For each cell, draw a 26-slot vertical "depth" bar representing the possible last characters used.
Draw a single column (Content). Draw a "Ruler" next to it. If the ruler marks > 15, highlight the row in green.

Problem Title: Count the Number of Consistent Strings
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 26-slot checklist. Check the "allowed" letters. Slide each word through a "stencil" with holes for those letters. If it fits, count it.

Problem Title: Sum of Absolute Differences in a Sorted Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a bar chart of the array. For any bar, the sum of differences is the area of the "empty space" to its left plus the area above it to its right.
Draw a 3-column table: Alice's Value | Bob's Value | Sum. Sort the rows by Sum (desc). Mark 'A' for the first row, 'B' for second, and so on.

Problem Title: Delivering Boxes from Storage to Ports
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line of boxes. Draw a "Truck Window" sliding over them. Maintain a "Trip Counter" that increments when consecutive boxes go to different ports.
Draw N dots. Circle two dots and draw an 'X' over one. Continue until only one dot is left without an 'X'.
Write the string (e.g., "3271"). Scan every character. Circle the largest digit.
Draw an N x N matrix. Fill the diagonals first (length 1), then length 2, then 3, until you reach the top-right corner.
Draw cuboids as sorted triplets. Draw arrows from smaller cuboids to larger ones they can support. Label each cuboid with its "max cumulative height".
Draw a grid (Candies vs. Bags). For each cell (n, k), show it pulling values from the cell above and the cell top-left.
Draw a table split into distinct color-coded boxes. In each box, list the IDs and circle only the unique ones.

Draw an array. Draw a rectangular "frame". If a number enters the frame that's already there, move the left side of the frame forward like a rubber band snapping.
Draw an array and a small "Tube" (Deque) above it. Write indices in the tube. When a new number comes, "kick out" smaller numbers from the back of the tube.

Problem Title: Checking Existence of Edge Length Limited Paths

Problem Title: Number of Distinct Substrings in a String

Problem Title: Number of Calls Between Two Persons (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with two columns. For each row, write the smaller ID on the left and larger on the right. Group the identical rows.

Problem Title: Minimum Adjacent Swaps for K Consecutive Ones

Problem Title: Maximum XOR With an Element From Array

How to Draw (Opt): Draw array and a box for Hash Map. Point to current element. Draw 22 branching arrows to potential "partners" in the Map.

Problem Title: Ways to Split Array Into Three Subarrays
Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sums + Binary Search (or Two Pointers).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw Prefix array. Pin index `i`. Draw a bracket representing the valid window `[j_min, j_max]` further down the array.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make a Subsequence
Optimised Pattern: Target Index Mapping + Longest Increasing Subsequence (LIS) via Patience Sorting.
How to Draw (Opt): Write target map. Rewrite `arr`. Draw "piles" of numbers. Place new numbers on leftmost valid pile (Binary Search). Count piles.

Problem Title: Sum Of Special Evenly-Spaced Elements In Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw queries hitting a toll booth. "Small" queries route to a precomputed lookup table. "Large" queries route to a manual loop.
Draw two tables side-by-side. Draw linking arrows. For missing links, draw a ghost box containing '0' snapping into place.
Draw large rectangular blocks for full weeks (AP formula). Draw a small staircase block for the remainder days. Add them.
Draw a vertical bucket (stack). Drop letters in. When "a" lands directly on "b", circle them, POOF them out, and write `+score`.

Problem Title: Construct the Lexicographically Largest Valid Sequence
Optimised Pattern: Backtracking + Greedy (Place largest available number first).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw blank slots. Hold the number 5. Drop it in slot 0. Count 5 steps right, drop the second 5. If blocked later, erase them and try 4.

Problem Title: Number Of Ways To Reconstruct A Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw nodes as circles sized by their degree. Largest circle goes at the top. Smaller circles hang off circles just slightly larger than them.

Optimised Pattern: Bitwise XOR Properties (`A ^ B = C` implies `A ^ C = B`).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw `ans[0]`. Draw an arrow from `ans[0]` and `encoded[0]` into an XOR gate (⊕), outputting `ans[1]`. Chain this forward continuously.

Problem Title: Swapping Nodes in a Linked List
How to Draw (Opt): Draw nodes. Drop an anchor at K. Connect a stick between head and K. Slide the stick right until the right end falls off. The left end is your target. Swap.

Optimised Pattern: Disjoint Set Union (DSU) / Connected Components + Hash Maps.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw indices as dots. Draw lines connecting allowed swaps. Circle the connected blobs. Next to each blob, tally available numbers. Cross them off as target uses them.

Problem Title: Find Minimum Time to Finish All Jobs
Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on Answer + Pruned Backtracking.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line at height T. Drop job blocks into K buckets. If a bucket overflows T, stop, undo, try next bucket. Prune heavily if dropping a block into an empty bucket fails.

Problem Title: Checking Existence of Edge Length Limited Paths II
Optimised Pattern: Kruskal's MST + LCA (Lowest Common Ancestor) with Binary Lifting.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw original graph. Re-draw it bottom-up: nodes merge into a parent circle labeled with the bottleneck edge weight. Trace paths up to common ancestor.

Problem Title: Number Of Rectangles That Can Form The Largest Square
Optimised Pattern: Single Pass Streaming Maximum (King of the Hill).

Draw pairs `(a,b)` mapping to a bucket labeled `Product X`. If two pairs land in the same bucket, draw arrows linking them into 8 different tuple arrangements.
Draw matrix of 1s and 0s. Transform columns into vertical stacks of blocks (tallest where 1s are continuous). On each row, sort the stacks tallest to shortest. Draw the largest bounding box.

Optimised Pattern: Game Theory (Minimax) + 3D Memoization (Topological Sort from End States).

Draw rows of data. Draw buckets labeled with `user_id`. Toss rows into their respective buckets. Tally the items in each bucket. Order buckets ascending.

Optimised Pattern: BFS (Breadth First Search) for Unweighted Shortest Path.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw grid. Place a dot at start. Draw concentric rings expanding outwards by 1 cell per step, ignoring walls. Stop when a ring touches food.

Problem Title: The Number of Employees Which Report to Each Employee (SQL)
Optimised Pattern: Self-Join on ID = Reports_To + Group By.

Draw a mountain profile. Write the 'gain' on the slopes (+5, -3). Label each peak with the current sum. Highlight the highest point.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of People to Teach
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two circles per friendship representing languages. If circles don't overlap, mark as 'Broken'. In a side tally, count how many broken-friendship-people know Language X.

Problem Title: Count Ways to Make Array With Product

Problem Title: Latest Time by Replacing Hidden Digits

Problem Title: Change Minimum Characters to Satisfy Condition
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two bar charts (A and B). Draw a vertical line between 'c' and 'c+1'. Shifting the line calculates cost in O(1).

Problem Title: Find Kth Largest XOR Coordinate Value
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a cell. Color its top neighbor, its left neighbor, and the diagonal. Use the 2D XOR formula to fill the grid.
Draw a 3D pyramid. Calculate its base. For leftovers, draw a new 2D triangle attached to the base.

Problem Title: Find Distance in a Binary Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the tree. Highlight `p` and `q`. Trace up to the first shared node (LCA). Count steps from `p` to LCA and `q` to LCA.

Problem Title: Find Total Time Spent by Each Employee (SQL)

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Balls in a Box
Optimised Pattern: Mathematical Digit Summing + Array Frequency Map.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sequence of numbers. Below each, draw a small machine that spits out a single sum (e.g., 15). Point an arrow to Box 15 and add a tick mark.

Problem Title: Restore the Array From Adjacent Pairs
Optimised Pattern: Graph Adjacency List + Degree Finding + DFS/Linear Traversal.

Problem Title: Can You Eat Your Favorite Candy on Your Favorite Day?

Draw an N x N grid. Fill it diagonally. Once filled, pick two split points `i` and `j`. Check three coordinates in your grid. If all are 'T', result is 'T'.

Problem Title: Maximum Subarray Sum After One Operation

Draw two horizontal bars for the same user but different IPs. If they overlap vertically at any point, circle the conflict.
Draw bins labeled with numbers found. Toss items into bins. Only count the sum of bins that contain exactly one item.

Problem Title: Maximum Absolute Sum of Any Subarray
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line graph of prefix sums. Find the highest peak and the lowest valley. The vertical distance between them is your answer.

Problem Title: Minimum Length of String After Deleting Similar Ends
How to Draw (Opt): Draw string. Place `L` at start, `R` at end. If they match 'a', move `L` past all 'a's and `R` past all 'a's. Stop when they don't match or they cross.

How to Draw (Opt): Draw timeline. Use a bracket for current event. Draw a dashed arrow skipping ahead to the first available event after the bracket ends.

Problem Title: Check if Array Is Sorted and Rotated

Optimised Pattern: Greedy (Always pick two largest) or Math.

Problem Title: Minimum Changes To Make Alternating Binary String
