How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Above it, draw the "Max Wall" growing left-to-right. Below, draw the "Min Wall" growing right-to-left. To check i, just look at the two walls directly above and below it.

Optimised Pattern: Hash Map Point Frequencies + Diagonal Anchoring
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a query point and one diagonal point. Draw a bounding box around them. The other two corners are instantly known. Read their counts from a Hash Map.

Optimised Pattern: Breadth-First Search (BFS) String Building + Frequency Pruning
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a queue. Start with single valid letters. Branch out to 2-letter combos. Cross out branches that fail the subsequence check immediately.

Problem Title: Average Height of Buildings in Each Segment
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line. Drop a green flag at a building's start, a red flag at its end. Walk the line, updating your "current total height" only at the flags.

Optimised Pattern: Single Pass with Running Minimum (Prefix Min)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Keep your left finger anchored on the smallest number seen so far. Move your right finger one by one, writing the difference below.

Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sum & Suffix Sum Arrays (Minimax pattern)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 2xN grid. Draw a solid line going right, then dropping at column `i`. Shade the remaining top-right and bottom-left cells. Compare their sums.

Problem Title: Check if Word Can Be Placed In Crossword
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Highlight a gap between two '#' blocks. Below it, write the target word forwards, then backwards. Connect matching letters with straight lines.

Problem Title: The Score of Students Solving Math Expression
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a pyramid. Bottom layer is single digits. Above layers combine digits using operators, storing sets of possible results, capping out at 1000.

Problem Title: Number of Accounts That Did Not Stream (SQL)
Optimised Pattern: LEFT JOIN with NULL check / EXCEPT operator
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two overlapping circles. Left is "2021 Subscriptions", Right is "2021 Streams". Shade ONLY the left crescent moon part.
Draw a number line. Mark start points with a green '+1' arrow pointing up, and end points with a red '-1' arrow pointing down. Tally the running total as you walk the line.
Write the 1D indices 0,1,2,3. If cols=2, write the division (row) and modulo (col) results underneath each index to see the exact 2D coordinates instantly.

Problem Title: Number of Pairs of Strings With Concatenation Equal to Target
How to Draw (Opt): Write the target string. Draw a vertical dashed line separating it into two parts. Look up both parts in a frequency table drawn to the side.

Problem Title: Maximize the Confusion of an Exam
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Use two fingers (L and R). Move R forward. Keep a tally of the "wrong" answer. If the tally exceeds `k`, drag L forward until it drops back to `k`.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Ways to Partition an Array
Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sums + Hash Maps for Difference Tracking
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a balance scale. The left side is Prefix, right is Suffix. When you change a number, write `+d` on the left side and `-d` on the right side. Check Hash Maps to re-balance.

Optimised Pattern: Direct SQL Filtering (Math inside WHERE clause)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table. Add a temporary column mentally for the calculation. Cross out rows >= 0.6. Read the remaining IDs top to bottom.
Write out the string. Put your finger at index 0. If it's 'O', move 1 step. If it's 'X', draw an arc jumping over the next 3 letters, and add a tally mark.
Draw `n` empty boxes. Calculate the base integer and write it in every box. Take the remainder and distribute it as "+1"s starting from the first box.
Draw 3 buckets labeled 0, 1, and 2. Tally the stones into them. Follow a simple 3-branch flowchart rule based on the tally counts to declare Alice or Bob the winner.

Problem Title: Smallest K-Length Subsequence With Occurrences of a Letter
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a string. Maintain two counters: total chars left, target chars left. Draw a stack (array). When a smaller char comes, erase the top of the stack if counters allow it.

Problem Title: Count Subarrays With More Ones Than Zeros
Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sum + Binary Indexed Tree (Fenwick) / Merge Sort
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line chart of the prefix sum going up and down. For every point, cast a horizontal ray to the left. Count how many points lie strictly below this ray.

Optimised Pattern: Frequency Hash Map or Bitmasking on an Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table from 1 to 100. Make three columns (A, B, C) with checkboxes. Tick the boxes as you iterate. Pick rows with at least 2 ticks.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make a Uni-Value Grid
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the 2D grid melting into a sorted 1D line. Put a star on the exact middle element (median). Draw arcs from all other numbers to the median, verifying step sizes.

Optimised Pattern: Hash Map (Timestamp -> Price) + Two Heaps (or TreeMap)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a Hash Map on the left for direct time lookups. On the right, draw a Min-Heap and a Max-Heap. Draw "trash cans" next to the heaps for lazy-deleting outdated prices.

Problem Title: Partition Array Into Two Arrays to Minimize Sum Difference
Optimised Pattern: Meet in the Middle + Binary Search
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two islands (Left and Right). On each island, sort subsets into buckets labeled by size (0 to N). Draw a bridge connecting a bucket of size `k` on the left to `N-k` on the right.
Draw two horizontal timelines: "Max Even Length" and "Max Odd Length". Walk through the array, drawing crossing arrows between the two lines to show state transitions.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Moves to Seat Everyone
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the unsorted arrays. Then draw them sorted directly beneath. Draw perfectly vertical, parallel lines connecting index 0 to 0, 1 to 1, etc., writing the difference on the line.

Problem Title: Remove Colored Pieces if Both Neighbors are the Same Color
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Circle blocks of 'A's. If a block has length L > 2, write L-2 below it for Alice. Do the same for Bob's 'B' blocks. See who has a higher total score.

Problem Title: The Time When the Network Becomes Idle
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the graph with node 0 at the top. Draw downward layers (Level 1, Level 2). Write the round-trip time next to each node, then apply the math formula to find its specific "idle time".

Problem Title: Kth Smallest Product of Two Sorted Arrays
Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on Answer Range + Two Pointers/Binary Search for Counting
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a massive number line from -10^10 to 10^10. Place a pin at `mid`. Below it, draw the two sorted arrays and show pointers sliding to count valid pairs without generating them.
Draw two tables merging into a smaller third table (JOIN). Draw a funnel at the bottom catching only candidates passing the `>15` and `>=2` filters.

Problem Title: Check if Numbers Are Ascending in a Sentence
How to Draw (Opt): Write the sentence. Use your finger as a playhead. Stop at a number. Compare it to a single sticky note labeled `prev`. Overwrite the sticky note and keep moving.
Draw an array of boxes. Label boxes 0 to N-1. When given Account 5, draw a lightning bolt directly to box 4. Instantly perform the +/- math.

Problem Title: Count Number of Maximum Bitwise-OR Subsets
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a binary tree. Each node is a decision (Take/Skip). Carry the accumulated OR value down the branches. At the bottom leaves, check if the value matches `max_or`.

Problem Title: Second Minimum Time to Reach Destination
Optimised Pattern: Modified BFS (Dijkstra-like) with Top-2 Tracking + Traffic Light Math
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the graph. Next to each node, draw two empty slots: `[ t1, t2 ]`. As BFS waves hit the node, fill t1. The next wave (or cyclic wave) fills t2. Stop when destination's t2 is filled.

Problem Title: Sort Linked List Already Sorted Using Absolute Values
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the list. When you hit a negative node, draw a dashed arrow showing it moving to the front. Draw a solid arrow updating the `previous.next` pointer.

Problem Title: Number of Valid Words in a Sentence
Optimised Pattern: Regex Matching or Structured Single-Pass Scan
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a word. Draw three boxes below: `hyphens`, `punctuation`, `valid?`. Fill boxes as you scan character by character. If a digit appears, immediately cross out the word.
Draw a tree where each branch is a valid digit count (e.g., three 3s). At the bottom, list the generated numbers like 122, 333, etc. Find where N fits in the list.

Problem Title: Count Nodes With the Highest Score
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the tree. Write the size of the subtree inside each node starting from the leaves up. At each node, multiply its children's sizes and the remaining outer nodes.
Draw nodes with their individual times. Draw arrows (prereqs). For each node, calculate: `StartTime = Max(Prereq Finish Times)`. The answer is `Max(StartTime + Duration)`.

Problem Title: The Category of Each Member in the Store (SQL)
Optimised Pattern: Left Join + CASE Statement + Aggregate Functions
How to Draw (Opt): Draw three circles (Venn Diagram). Use a Left Join to keep all Members. Group items into buckets. Apply a formula label to each bucket based on the percentage result.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost to Separate Sentence Into Rows
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the words in a line. Underneath, draw an array `dp`. From each cell `i`, draw arcs to future cells `j` that stay within the `k` limit. Choose the cheapest arc.

Problem Title: Kth Distinct String in an Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array on the left and a Hash Map on the right. Tally everything. Then, walk the array again, pointing to the Map. If the tally is '1', tick a "K counter".
Draw a timeline. Above it, sorted intervals. Below it, a `suffix_max` line. For an interval, draw an arrow to the right, jumping over its end time to find the highest value waiting there.

Draw the string. Below it, draw three helper rows: `LeftCandleIdx`, `RightCandleIdx`, and `PlateCount`. For a query, draw two lightning bolts to the helper rows to get the bounds and subtract the counts.

Problem Title: Number of Valid Move Combinations On Chessboard
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Level 1 = Piece 1 moves. Level 2 = Piece 2 moves. At each node, draw a mini-board and mark "X" if paths intersect in time.
Write indices 0 to N. Below index 0, write "0 % 10 = 0?". If it matches `nums[0]`, stop. Draw a single forward arrow.

Problem Title: Find the Minimum and Maximum Number of Nodes Between Critical Points
Optimised Pattern: Single Pass with Three-Node Window + Edge Point Tracking
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the list. Use a 3-node bracket `[prev, curr, next]`. When a CP is found, draw a pin on the timeline. Update `min` using the last pin and `max` using the very first pin.
Draw a number line 0 to 1000. Start a ripple at `start`. Show the ripple expanding to new numbers each step. If a ripple hits `goal`, stop.

Problem Title: Check if an Original String Exists Given Two Encoded Strings
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two strings. Draw a "Delta Gauge" (a scale). Read a number? Add weight to the scale. Read a letter? Subtract weight. If the scale stays near 0 and letters match, you are valid.

Problem Title: Number of Spaces Cleaned by Robot
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. As the robot cleans a cell, shade it. On the side, maintain a "State Log": `(0,0,East) -> (0,1,East)`. If you see a log entry repeat, stop.

Problem Title: Count Vowel Substrings of a String
Optimised Pattern: Sliding Window (Two Pointers) with Sub-problem Logic
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Mark consonant positions as "walls". Between walls, slide a window. Tally vowel counts in a 5-slot box below the window.
Write the string. For each vowel, draw an arrow pointing left (start options) and right (end options). Write the math `(i+1)*(n-i)` underneath it. Sum the results.

Problem Title: Minimized Maximum of Products Distributed to Any Store
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line `[1 ... 100,000]`. Mark `mid`. Below it, write the product array and divide each by `mid`. If the total "buckets" fit in `n`, shade the left half.

Problem Title: Maximum Path Quality of a Graph
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree where each node is a graph node. Beside each node, write `remTime`. If `remTime < 0`, cross out the branch. Keep a "Visited Set" for quality tally.

Optimised Pattern: SQL Common Table Expressions (CTE) + SUM() Aggregate
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table of transactions. Group them by ID. Calculate a "Net Change" column. Draw an arrow merging this with the original "Starting Balance".
Write the string. For `v=1`, slide a bracket of size `count`. For `v=2`, slide a bracket of size `2*count`. Check counts in the bracket.

Problem Title: Check Whether Two Strings are Almost Equivalent
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two 26-slot boxes labeled A-Z. As you read string1, tally marks in box 1. For string2, tally in box 2. Finally, subtract the tallies for each slot.
Draw the grid. Calculate the perimeter length. If the robot moves past the total length, use the remainder. Mark the remainder's position on the "border" only.

Problem Title: Most Beautiful Item for Each Query
How to Draw (Opt): Draw items as dots on a graph (Price vs Beauty). Connect dots with a "staircase" line that never goes down. For a query, draw a vertical line and find where it hits the staircase.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Tasks You Can Assign
Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on Answer + Greedy with Monotonic Queue (Deque)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two sorted arrays. Pick the k smallest tasks and k largest workers. Draw arrows from workers to tasks. If a worker needs a pill, draw a '+' sign and jump to the hardest possible task they can now handle.
Draw two tally boxes labeled NY and CA. Put a checkmark in NY for every score >= 90. Do the same for CA. Compare the final numbers on a podium.
Write the array. Circle the value at index k. For every element to the left, write `min(val, tickets[k])`. For elements to the right, write `min(val, tickets[k]-1)`. Sum the new numbers.

Problem Title: Reverse Nodes in Even Length Groups
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the list. Use a bracket to mark groups of increasing size. If a bracket has an even number of nodes, draw a "U-turn" arrow inside it and update the `.next` pointers on both ends.
Write the string. Calculate the `cols` value. For the first diagonal, circle index 0, then `cols + 1`, then `2*(cols + 1)`. For the second diagonal, start at 1, then `cols + 2`.

Optimised Pattern: Disjoint Set Union (DSU) with Restrictions Check
How to Draw (Opt): Draw circles (components). For each request, draw a "tentative bridge". Check the "Forbidden List". If no forbidden pair exists across the bridge, solidify the line.

Problem Title: Paths in Maze That Lead to Same Room
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an adjacency list as sets. For edge (u, v), highlight the overlap between `Set(u)` and `Set(v)`. Every highlighted number is a valid 3-cycle.

Problem Title: Two Furthest Houses With Different Colors
How to Draw (Opt): Write the array. Place your left finger on index 0. Slide your right finger from the end until it hits a different color. Repeat with right finger on N-1 and slide left.
Draw the plant line. Draw a tally marks for each forward step. At a refill, draw a long U-turn arrow back to the start and back to the current plant. Write the math below.

Optimised Pattern: Hash Map of Sorted Indices + Binary Search
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a Hash Map where keys are values. Next to each key, draw a sorted row of indices. For a query, use two "binary search" arrows to find the range within that row. Result is `end_idx - start_idx + 1`.
Draw a "root" half-number (e.g., '12'). Fold it over a dashed line to get '1221'. Immediately convert '1221' to base-k and verify the string.
Draw the table. Cross out rows <= 500. Highlight the IDs in the remaining rows, but only circle each unique ID once. Write the total count at the bottom.

Problem Title: Substrings That Begin and End With the Same Letter
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Draw buckets for A-Z. Read 'a'. Add 1 to 'a' bucket. Add bucket's value to Total. Read 'b'. Add 1 to 'b' bucket. Add to Total.

Problem Title: Drop Type 1 Orders for Customers With Type 0 Orders (SQL)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw groups of orders. For a group containing both 0 and 1, draw a cross over the 1s. For a group with only 1s, keep them.

Problem Title: Count Common Words With One Occurrence
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two Hash Maps. Fill them with tallies. Highlight keys where both maps show a count of exactly "1".

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Food Buckets to Feed the Hamsters
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. When you hit a Hamster ('H'), look right. If it's a '.', draw a Bucket ('B') there. This bucket projects a feeding aura to i and i+2.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost Homecoming of a Robot in a Grid
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the X-axis and Y-axis costs as bars. Shade all bars between startRow and homeRow, and startCol and homeCol. Sum the shaded areas.

Problem Title: Count Fertile Pyramids in a Land
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid. Fill it with numbers. Each number is the min of its three "supports" below plus 1. Do it twice: once for up, once for down.

Problem Title: Find Target Indices After Sorting Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw two boxes: `countSmaller` and `countEqual`. Every number increments one of them. The result is `[smaller, smaller + 1, ... , smaller + equal - 1]`.
Draw the array. Place a bracket of size 2k+1. As you move the bracket, draw a minus sign on the left edge and a plus sign on the right edge.

Problem Title: Removing Minimum and Maximum From Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Mark indices i and j. Draw three arrows: one from left to the furthest index, one from right to the furthest, and two shorter arrows from both ends.

Optimised Pattern: Sorted Time Processing + DSU with Reset
How to Draw (Opt): Draw groups of meetings by time. Inside each group, draw DSU "bubbles". If a bubble doesn't touch the "Secret Person", pop the bubble (reset parents) before moving to the next time.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost to Reach City With Discounts
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the cities. Above each city, draw "Discount Levels" 0 to K. Draw arrows between cities that either stay on the same level or drop down one level.
Draw a list: 100, 102, 104... 998. Draw a single digit frequency tally of the input array. For '102', ask: "Do I have one 1, one 0, and one 2?".

Problem Title: Delete the Middle Node of a Linked List
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the list. Use two markers. Move one twice as fast as the other. When the fast one hits the finish line, the slow one is exactly at the node before the middle.

Problem Title: Step-By-Step Directions From a Binary Tree Node to Another
How to Draw (Opt): Draw paths from root to S and D. Circle the fork in the road (LCA). Replace every edge in the S-branch with 'U' (Up) and keep the D-branch edges as is.
Draw the graph nodes. Start at the "Source". Follow edges, erasing them as you go. If you get stuck, move the node to a "Finished" list and step back.

Problem Title: Subsequence of Size K With the Largest Even Sum
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sorted list. Draw a vertical line after K items. If sum is odd, draw arrows showing potential "trades" across the line to flip parity.

Problem Title: Find Subsequence of Length K With the Largest Sum
Optimised Pattern: Sorting by Value + Sorting by Index
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Circle the K largest numbers regardless of position. Then, rewrite those circled numbers in the order they appeared in the original string.

Problem Title: Find Good Days to Rob the Bank
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two helper rows. `Row 1`: count how long the price has been falling. `Row 2`: count how long the price will stay rising. Highlight indices where both rows have values >= `time`.
Draw circles for bombs, draw one-way arrows if bomb A radius covers center of bomb B.

Optimised Pattern: Two Heaps (Min-Heap / Max-Heap) or Balanced BST.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 2 triangles (trees). Draw arrows moving root of one triangle over to the bottom of the other.
Draw 10 slots. In each slot write 000. Cross out 0s and write 1s based on RGB colors seen.
Draw a U-shape (stack). Draw numbers dropping in. If a smaller number drops in, draw the larger ones getting kicked out.
Draw water bucket icons above A and B. Cross out bucket capacity and write new capacity after each plant.

Problem Title: Maximum Fruits Harvested After at Most K Steps
Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sum + Binary Search (or Sliding Window).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line. Mark starting point. Draw an arched arrow going left, then turning back right, highlighting the covered segment.

Problem Title: Number of Unique Flavors After Sharing K Candies
How to Draw (Opt): Draw array. Draw bracket [ ... ] of size K. Erase old boundary, draw new boundary shifted by 1. Update tally marks in a box.

Problem Title: Find First Palindromic String in the Array
How to Draw (Opt): Word written out. Draw arrows pointing to first and last letter. Move them towards the center step-by-step.
Old string at top, new longer blank array at bottom. Draw lines mapping old letters to new slots, inserting ' ' where indices match.

Problem Title: Number of Smooth Descent Periods of a Stock
How to Draw (Opt): Line graph of stock prices. Draw triangles under the descending slopes. The area is the total periods.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make the Array K-Increasing
Optimised Pattern: Longest Non-Decreasing Subsequence (Patience Sorting / Binary Search).
How to Draw (Opt): Circle elements spaced by `k`. Draw a separate line for them. Cross out elements that break the increasing order.

Problem Title: The Airport With the Most Traffic
How to Draw (Opt): Draw airports as nodes. Every flight is an edge. Write a number inside the node that goes up by 1 for every touching edge.

Problem Title: Elements in Array After Removing and Replacing Elements
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a V-shape. Top-left is full array, bottom is empty, top-right is full again. Map the `time` to a point on this V to find array size.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Words Found in Sentences
How to Draw (Opt): Write the sentences. Put a dot under every space. Tally the dots + 1.

Problem Title: Find All Possible Recipes from Given Supplies
How to Draw (Opt): Draw circles for supplies (green) and recipes (white). Draw arrows from ingredients to recipes. Erase arrow when ingredient is available. Color recipe green when 0 arrows remain.

Problem Title: Check if a Parentheses String Can Be Valid
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Below it, write a running sum tracking `minOpen` and `maxOpen`. Cross out invalid paths if `maxOpen` drops below 0.

Problem Title: Abbreviating the Product of a Range
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a long rectangle. Shade the left 5 blocks (Prefix) and right 5 blocks (Suffix). Cross out the middle and draw an ellipsis (...). Add a bucket for trailing zeros.
Draw a 2-column table (Power, Factor). Draw an arrow pulling the highest power row first, writing it on a line, then the next, forming an equation.

Problem Title: A Number After a Double Reversal
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number. Look at the last digit. If it's a '0' (and the number isn't just '0'), draw a red 'X' because the zero will vanish when reversed.

Problem Title: Execution of All Suffix Instructions Staying in a Grid
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 3x3 grid. Draw an 'X' at start. Trace the path `R, R, L, U`. Count steps. Erase the path. Start again from the second instruction `R, L, U`.

Optimised Pattern: Prefix and Suffix Sums / Hash Map of Lists.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw out a sub-array of indices for a specific number. Draw a left-facing arrow calculating left-side pull, and a right-facing arrow for right-side pull. Add them.
Draw sorted array. Draw an arrow from index 0 to index `i`. Calculate `diff`. Go through the rest of the array crossing off numbers that are exactly `diff` apart.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Remove Adjacent Ones in Matrix
Optimised Pattern: Bipartite Graph Matching / Min Vertex Cover.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Circle the 1s. Color them alternating shades. Draw lines between adjacent 1s. Circle the minimum number of nodes needed to break all those lines.

Problem Title: Check if All A's Appears Before All B's
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Slide a box of size 2 across it. If the box ever contains "ba", draw a red X and stop.

Problem Title: Number of Laser Beams in a Bank
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Write the total count of '1's next to each row. Cross out any '0' totals. Multiply the remaining adjacent numbers: `R1 * R2 + R2 * R3`.
Draw sorted rocks in a line left to right. Draw a Pac-Man eating them one by one, writing its new size above each eaten rock. If it hits a wall (bigger rock), draw an X.

Problem Title: Maximum Employees to Be Invited to a Meeting
Optimised Pattern: Graph (Functional Graph / Cycle Detection + Topological Sort).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw nodes as circles, with single arrows pointing to their favorite. Find closed loops. For 2-node loops, draw the longest "tail" of nodes pointing into each half of the pair.

Problem Title: Remove All Ones With Row and Column Flips
How to Draw (Opt): Write out Row 0. Write Row 1 below it. Check if R1 == R0 or R1 == ~R0. If not, draw a giant red X. It's impossible. Repeat for all rows.

Optimised Pattern: String Builder / Two Pointers (In-place if allowed).
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Box each word. If the box length is 1 or 2, do nothing. If > 2, circle the first letter and write its capital above it.

Problem Title: Maximum Twin Sum of a Linked List
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the list. Find the middle line. Erase the arrows in the second half and draw them pointing backward. Draw a curved line bringing the old tail directly under the head.

Problem Title: Longest Palindrome by Concatenating Two Letter Words
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 2-letter blocks. Find a block's reverse, draw a line connecting them, and cross both out. If a block has identical letters (e.g., "zz"), leave one uncrossed for the absolute middle.
Draw the grid. Draw an overlay calculating blocked sums. If sum == 0 for a stamp area, draw +1 at top-left and -1 just outside bottom-right. Resolve the +1s and -1s to shade covered areas.

Problem Title: Check if Every Row and Column Contains All Numbers
Optimised Pattern: Hash Set Validation (or Boolean Array Tracking).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Next to Row 1, draw a box checking off numbers 1-N. Above Col 1, draw another box. If you write the same number twice in a box, draw a red X.

Problem Title: Minimum Swaps to Group All 1's Together II
Optimised Pattern: Fixed Sliding Window on a Concatenated Array.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array twice side-by-side. Draw a rectangle of size `k`. Slide it to the right one step at a time. Keep track of the highest number of 1s you see inside it.

Problem Title: Count Words Obtained After Adding a Letter
How to Draw (Opt): Write target word. Sort its letters. Draw lines branching down where one letter is omitted. If the resulting word is in the "Start Words Box", circle it in green.

Problem Title: Earliest Possible Day of Full Bloom
How to Draw (Opt): Draw horizontal bars. Red for planting (must be sequential), Green for growing (can overlap). Place the longest green bars first to let them grow while you plant the rest.

Problem Title: Pour Water Between Buckets to Make Water Levels Equal
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an uneven bar chart. Draw a horizontal line across the middle. Shade the tops of bars above the line (water to pour) and the empty space below the line (water needed). Balance the shaded areas.

Problem Title: Divide a String Into Groups of Size k
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Draw vertical lines every `k` characters. For the very last box, if there are empty slots, draw the `fill` character to pad it out.

Problem Title: Minimum Moves to Reach Target Score
How to Draw (Opt): Write the target number. If it's even, draw a long arrow dividing it by 2. If it's odd, draw a tiny arrow subtracting 1. Stop when you hit 1.
Draw an array. Start at the last box. Write its points. Move left. For each box, draw an arrow to the very next box, and another arched arrow skipping `brainpower` boxes. Take the max sum.

Problem Title: Maximum Running Time of N Computers
How to Draw (Opt): Draw `n` vertical columns (computers). Draw batteries as blocks. Any battery taller than the `mid` line is "chopped off" at the line. Sum the areas of all blocks and see if it covers the rectangle `n * mid`.

Problem Title: The Number of Passengers in Each Bus (SQL)
Optimised Pattern: Sorting + Window Functions (Lead/Lag) or Inequality Joins.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a horizontal time axis. Mark buses as vertical walls. Mark passengers as dots. Count how many dots are trapped between two walls.

Problem Title: Choose Numbers From Two Arrays in Range
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line with 0 in the center. At each step, move all existing "counts" to the left by `b` and to the right by `a`. Sum overlapping counts.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost of Buying Candies With Discount
How to Draw (Opt): Draw candies in a row from largest to smallest. Circle 2, cross out 1. Circle 2, cross out 1. Sum the circled ones.

Draw a mountain range (relative sequence). Measure the distance from the highest peak to the lowest valley. See how many ways this "mountain" can slide up and down between the `upper` and `lower` lines.

Problem Title: K Highest Ranked Items Within a Price Range
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid. Mark 'Start'. Draw concentric squares (distance 1, 2, 3). For items found, write their (dist, price, r, c) in a "Top K" table, replacing entries as higher ranks appear.

Problem Title: Number of Ways to Divide a Long Corridor
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 'S' and 'P' on a line. Circle groups of two 'S'. Look at the empty space between circles. Count the 'P's in that gap. `Ways = (P1+1) * (P2+1)...`

Problem Title: Count Elements With Strictly Smaller and Greater Elements
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line. Mark the absolute leftmost (Min) and rightmost (Max) points. Count all dots that fall strictly between these two lines.
Draw the empty result array with indices 0, 1, 2, 3... Point a finger at 0 (for Pos) and 1 (for Neg). Move through input; fill the slot your finger is on, then hop that finger two spaces right.

How to Draw (Opt): Draw a row of people. Use a stack to keep track of the "tallest seen so far." When a person is popped, increment their "seen" count. Show how each person enters and leaves the stack exactly once.

Problem Title: Check if Number Has Equal Digit Count and Digit Value

Draw the array. Use a stack to track how long each element survives. When an element is blocked by a larger one to its left, calculate its "survival duration" based on the neighbors it outlasted.

Problem Title: Minimum Obstacle Removal to Reach Corner

Draw a 1D array representing budget levels. For a stock with cost 5 and profit 10, show the values in the array from index 5 to Max shifting upwards as the new profit is added.

Draw a flow chart. Data enters, passes through a "Weekend Filter" (Saturday/Sunday), and increments a counter. Everything else passes into a "Weekday Counter."

Problem Title: Paths in Matrix Whose Sum Is Divisible by K

Problem Title: Minimum Split Into Subarrays With GCD Greater Than One

Draw the array. Use three colored arrows (Red for invalid, Blue for minK, Green for maxK). At each step, count = max(0, min(Blue, Green) - Red).

Problem Title: Number of Nodes With Value One
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the tree. Write query counts next to nodes. Draw an arrow dropping from root to leaves, accumulating the sum. If sum is odd, write '1'.

How to Draw (Opt): Draw two horizontal lines. Label Start/End for each. Check if the start of one is before the end of the other and vice-versa.

Problem Title: Number of Subarrays With GCD Equal to K
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Under index 'i', draw a chain of GCD results. Stop drawing the chain as soon as a number < K appears or GCD becomes < K.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost to Make Array Equal

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Operations to Make Arrays Similar

Problem Title: Number of Distinct Binary Strings After Operations
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a string. Draw a sliding box of length K. Count how many positions the box can start in. Each position is a bit in a new virtual string.
Draw a bucket for each unique pattern you see. Drop the strings into the buckets. Pick the string from the bucket that has only one occupant.

Problem Title: Words Within Two Edits of Dictionary

Draw a set of buckets labeled 0, 1, ... (space-1). Drop each number into the bucket corresponding to its remainder. The largest bucket wins.
Draw two vertical bins. When a new number is larger than Bin1's top, move the top to Bin2. If larger than Bin2's top, it's the 2nd greater; write it down.

Problem Title: Average Value of Even Numbers Divisible by Three
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a single loop. Inside, draw two boxes: `S` (sum) and `C` (count). Add to them only if `n % 6 == 0`.
Draw a table with columns: Creator, TotalViews, BestVideo, BestVideoViews. Update rows as you process each entry. Highlight rows with the highest TotalViews.

Problem Title: Beautiful Number / Minimum Addition to Make Integer Beautiful
How to Draw (Opt): Write the number. Circle the rightmost non-zero digit. Change it to 0 and add 1 to the digit to its left. Re-check the digit sum. Repeat.

Problem Title: Height of Binary Tree After Subtree Removal Queries

Problem Title: Sort Array by Moving Items to Empty Space
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw an arrow from `nums[i]` to where it belongs. The arrows form loops. Count the nodes in each closed loop.
Draw the array. Use an 'R' (Read) and 'W' (Write) arrow. If R sees a number, move it to W and increment both. If R sees 0, only R moves.

Problem Title: Maximum Sum of Distinct Subarrays With Length K
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array with a box of size K. Write the running sum above. Maintain a list of counts for the numbers inside the box. Slide box right.

Problem Title: Total Cost to Hire K Workers
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two inverted trees (Heaps). Draw an array with two pointers moving toward the center. Show nodes moving from array to trees.
Draw a grid where rows = Robots, cols = Factories. Fill cells by choosing between "Skip factory" or "Use factory for X robots."

Problem Title: Minimum Subarrays in a Valid Split
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. For each index, draw backward-pointing arrows to indices where the GCD condition is met. Write the min-cost inside each array cell.
Draw a sorted array. Draw an arrow at index 0 and index N-1. Move them toward the center one step at a time, writing the sum of the pointed values.

Problem Title: Count Ways To Build Good Strings

Write the equation: `k * limit - overhead >= len(msg)`. Draw a table of overheads for ranges (1-9, 10-99, etc.).
Draw a box with 'Celsius' entering and an array `[K, F]` exiting. Write the formulas inside the box.

Problem Title: Number of Subarrays With LCM Equal to K
Optimised Pattern: Optimized Brute Force with LCM Early Exit

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Operations to Sort a Binary Tree by Level

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Non-overlapping Palindrome Substrings

Draw a giant cloud (Super Source). Draw arrows dropping from the cloud down to every city holding an apple. Run the pathfinding outwards from there.
Draw a timeline. Write purchase totals above years. Draw a minus sign between adjacent years. If the result is positive AND years are consecutive, it passes.

Problem Title: Number of Unequal Triplets in Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a frequency table. Draw three boxes: "Past", "Current", "Future". As you iterate through the table, slide frequencies between boxes and multiply.

Problem Title: Closest Nodes Queries in a Binary Search Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Below it, draw a straight line (array) where the tree nodes fall into place. Mark the query point 'x' on the line and identify neighbors.

Problem Title: Minimum Fuel Cost to Report to the Capital

Problem Title: Maximum XOR of Two Non-Overlapping Subtrees

Draw a column of Metals and a row of Non-Metals. Draw a grid where they intersect, forming the bonds.

Problem Title: Minimum Cuts to Divide a Circle
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a circle. Draw 4 lines (8 pieces). Draw a circle. Draw 3 lines (3 pieces, center-to-edge).

Draw a triangle representing the sum. Cut it. Solve `x^2 = n(n+1)/2`.

Optimised Pattern: Inverse Sliding Window (Two Pointers) seeking the maximum middle subarray to *leave behind*.

Optimised Pattern: Sort the array + Binary Search on the Answer Space + Greedy Checking.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sorted array. Write a target gap `G`. Circle the first number. Scan right until you find a number >= first + `G`. Circle it. Repeat. Count circled items. If count >= K, increase `G`.

Optimised Pattern: 0/1 Knapsack Dynamic Programming (Inverse Problem). Find subsets summing to strictly less than K, and subtract from total subsets.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a row of K boxes (indices 0 to K-1). Set box 0 to 1 (one way to make sum 0). Pick an array number `num`. Starting from the right of your boxes, add the value from index `i - num` to index `i`.

Problem Title: Count the Number of K-Big Indices
Optimised Pattern: Two Passes (Prefix and Suffix) using Max-Heaps of size K.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw a "Heap Box" that holds max K items. Sweep left-to-right. Write the max item in the box above the current array number. Sweep right-to-left doing the same. Circle indices where the array number is larger than both written maxes.

Problem Title: Count the Digits That Divide a Number
How to Draw (Opt): Write `num = 124`. Write `temp = 124`. Look at the last digit (4). Does 124 % 4 == 0? Yes, tally 1. Cross out the 4. Now `temp = 12`. Repeat.

Problem Title: Distinct Prime Factors of Product of Array
Optimised Pattern: On-the-fly Trial Division + Hash Set deduplication.

Problem Title: Partition String Into Substrings With Values at Most K
Optimised Pattern: Greedy Algorithm / String Parsing with continuous accumulation.
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Slide your finger over the numbers, reading the growing number aloud. When the spoken number is bigger than K, draw a vertical line before the last digit, tally 1, and resume reading from the line.

Optimised Pattern: Sieve of Eratosthenes (Precomputation) + Linear Array Scan.
How to Draw (Opt): Write numbers 1 to right in a grid. Cross out multiples. Write the remaining uncrossed primes in a list. Draw a jumping arrow between them, writing down the difference. Keep the smallest difference.

Problem Title: Maximum Frequency Score of a Subarray
Optimised Pattern: Fixed Sliding Window + Persistent Hash Map (or Array Cache) for O(1) incremental score updates.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Put a physical, bounded box over K elements. Keep a tally sheet beside it. Slide the box right by 1 index. Add a tally for the new number, erase a tally for the old left number.

Optimised Pattern: Pre-evaluated Boolean Variables mapping to strict logic combinations.
How to Draw (Opt): Write Bulky = [True/False] . Write Heavy = [True/False] . Look at the two answers and immediately pick the category based on the word combination without re-doing the math.

Problem Title: Find Consecutive Integers from a Data Stream
Optimised Pattern: State Machine / Single Counter tracking contiguous valid states.
How to Draw (Opt): Write the target `value` and target `k`. Draw a box for your `count`. As a number arrives, if it matches, draw a tally mark. If it fails, erase all tallies. If tallies reach `k`, you win.

Optimised Pattern: Mathematical Bitwise Cancellation (Symmetry). The expression simplifies to just XORing all elements in the array.
How to Draw (Opt): Write the array of numbers. Put a `^` (XOR) symbol between all of them. Calculate them left to right like a simple chain of dominoes.

Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on the Answer Space + Greedy Sliding Window.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line for the possible answers. Pick a middle number. Do a linear sweep across the cities, adding "temporary" stations when needed. If you run out of stations, cross out the right half of the number line.

Problem Title: Maximum Count of Positive Integer and Negative Integer
Optimised Pattern: Two separate Binary Searches (Upper Bound for negatives, Lower Bound for positives) leveraging the sorted nature of the array.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Pick the middle element. If it's negative, jump halfway to the right. If positive, jump halfway to the left. Pinpoint exactly where the numbers switch from negative to 0 to positive.

Problem Title: Maximal Score After Applying K Operations
Optimised Pattern: Max-Heap (Priority Queue) to maintain the largest element dynamically.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a pyramid of numbers with the biggest on top. Take the top number, add it to your score, divide it by 3, and drop it back into the pyramid, letting it sink to its proper level. Repeat.

Problem Title: Make Number of Distinct Characters Equal
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two frequency tables (26 rows). Pick a row from Table 1 and a row from Table 2. Mentally decrement one, increment the other, and count how many rows have a value > 0.

Draw a row of boxes from 0 to maxLen . Box 0 = 1. For each box i , look back zero steps and one steps. Add those two numbers and write the result in box i .

Problem Title: Time Taken to Cross the Door
Optimised Pattern: Two Queues (Enter and Exit) with State Tracking.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two columns: "Enter" and "Exit". Place person IDs in them sorted by arrival time. Use a "Last State" variable (0, 1, or Idle). Pop from the top of the correct column based on the state.

Problem Title: Difference Between Element Sum and Digit Sum of an Array
Optimised Pattern: Single-Pass Accumulation with Modulo Digit Extraction.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two columns: "Element Sum" and "Digit Sum". For each number in the array, write the full number in column 1, and write its individual digits in column 2. Add both columns and subtract.
Draw a grid. Put a '+1' at the top-left of the query box and a '-1' just outside the top-right. Do a running sum across the row to "fill" it, then a running sum down the columns.

Problem Title: Count the Number of Good Subarrays
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Slide a bracket `[ ]`. Keep a tally of pairs. When tally >= k, calculate (N - right) and add to total. Move the left bracket and repeat.

Problem Title: Difference Between Maximum and Minimum Price Sum
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. At each node, write two numbers. The first is the max price-sum including a leaf, the second is excluding a leaf. Combine these as you bubble up from children to parent.

How to Draw (Opt): Write "a:3, b:2". Pick frequency f=1 . Ways = (Comb(3,1)+1) * (Comb(2,1)+1) - 1 . Repeat for f=2 and f=3 . Sum the results.
Draw two sorted arrays. Put one finger on the start of each. Move the finger pointing to the smaller number. If they point to the same number, you found it.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make Array Equal II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with 3 columns: nums1 , nums2 , and Difference . Mark each difference as a multiple of k. Check if the sum of all differences is 0. If yes, count the "up-moves" (+ differences).

Write down x and y. Perform the Euclidean algorithm (repeated subtraction/modulo) to find the GCD. Once you have the GCD, check if it can be repeatedly divided by 2 until it becomes 1.
Write the number. Peel off the last digit. Write it with a minus. Peel next, write with a plus. If you end on a minus but it was the first digit, flip all signs in your head.

Problem Title: Sort the Students by Their Kth Score
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the matrix. Look only at column k. Identify the largest number in that column—move that entire row to the top. Identify the second largest—move that row to second. Repeat.

Problem Title: Apply Bitwise Operations to Make Strings Equal
How to Draw (Opt): Write s and target . Simply circle any '1' you see in both. If both have at least one circle, or neither has a circle, it's possible.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost to Split an Array
Optimised Pattern: 1D Dynamic Programming with Frequency Map optimization.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of boxes for dp . At box i , look back at all previous boxes j . Calculate the cost of the segment from j to i , add it to dp[j] , and pick the smallest result for dp[i] .

Problem Title: Maximum Price to Fill a Bag (Premium)
How to Draw (Opt): Write items as fractions: Price / Weight . Sort them. Pick the biggest fraction, subtract its weight from your bag's capacity. If it doesn't fit, multiply the fraction by your remaining capacity.
Write n. Note that n-1 always fits the rule. Then n-2 fits for n-1. Draw a line of numbers falling like dominoes.

How to Draw (Opt): Write 2^n. Subtract 2. Apply modulo. Use "Square and Multiply" to calculate 2^n on paper.

Problem Title: Separate the Digits in an Array
