How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Place a finger on the start. Move right; if the next number is +1, draw a "+" above it. When you have K-1 "+" marks in a row, circle the current number as the result.

Problem Title: Find the Power of K-Size Subarrays II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the input array. Below it, draw a "Streak" array where you write how many consecutive increasing numbers end at that index. For any window, just check if `Streak[end] >= k`.

Problem Title: Maximum Value Sum by Placing Three Rooks I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. For each row, circle the 3 largest numbers and cross out the rest. Now, pick 3 circled numbers such that no two are in the same column.

Problem Title: Maximum Value Sum by Placing Three Rooks II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Highlight row 'i'. Draw a "Max-So-Far" box above row 'i' and another "Max-So-Far" box below it. Pick the best from the top box, row 'i', and bottom box.

Problem Title: Count Substrings That Satisfy K-Constraint I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the binary string. Place two pointers (L, R). Slide R right. If you hit k+1 zeros and k+1 ones, move L right until one condition is met. Add the distance (R-L+1) to your total.

Problem Title: Maximum Energy Boost From Two Drinks
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two parallel rows of numbers. Use your left finger for A and right for B. To calculate A[i], look at the cell directly left (A[i-1]) and the one diagonal-left-twice (B[i-2]). Take the max.

Problem Title: Find the Largest Palindrome Divisible by K
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table where rows are "digit positions from edges" and columns are "remainders 0 to K-1". Mark 'True' if a remainder is reachable. Trace back from `dp[mid][0]` to find the digits.

Problem Title: Count Substrings That Satisfy K-Constraint II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Plot a line graph showing the distance `i - L[i] + 1` for each index. A query is a "slice" of this graph. Use prefix sums to find the "area" under the line in that slice.
Draw a number line of primes. Square them. Drop these "Special" tokens onto the main range line [L, R]. Count the tokens. Subtract from the total length.

Problem Title: Convert Doubly Linked List to Array II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the DLL. Put a pen on the head. Move the pen following `next` arrows, writing the value in an array at each step.

Problem Title: Final Array State After K Multiplication Operations I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a triangle (Heap). Point to the root (min). Show it being removed and replaced by the last node. Draw arrows for the "bubble down" and "bubble up" logic.
Draw a number "123". Draw lines to its neighbors: "213", "321", "132". Store these in a box (HashMap). Check if the target number is already in that box.

Problem Title: Final Array State After K Multiplication Operations II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Simulate a few steps until all numbers are roughly equal. Draw a "Fast Forward" arrow that jumps through the remaining 10^9 steps using a single math formula.

Optimised Pattern: Optimized BFS / DFS on Digit States
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number. Draw 1st-level swap neighbors. From those, draw 2nd-level swap neighbors. Use a "Seen" set for this specific number before adding to the global "Global" counter.

Plot points on a graph. Lock a top-left point. Scan rightwards. Track the "highest point seen so far." If the next point is below your top-left but *above* the highest point seen, the box between them is empty.

Draw a matrix with nums1 on the rows and nums2 on the columns. For each cell, draw arrows from the cell above and the cell to the left. Calculate the min value based on the previous cell and the current element's parity/magnitude.

Problem Title: Find the Key of the Numbers
How to Draw (Opt): Draw three numbers. Underneath, draw 4 rows (thousands, hundreds, tens, units). Tally the digits for each number into these rows and circle the winner in each.
Draw the string as a timeline. Draw brackets of width K. For each bracket, draw an arrow pointing to a single letter result below it.

Problem Title: Find the Count of Good Integers
How to Draw (Opt): Draw half the digits (e.g., N=4, draw slots 1 and 2). Branch digits 0-9. Reflect to form a palindrome. If divisible by K, list its digit frequencies (e.g., {1:2, 2:2}). Use the formula to count unique arrangements.

Problem Title: Minimum Amount of Damage Dealt to Bob
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with columns: Damage, Time. Add a third column: Ratio (D/T). Sort the table. Highlight that killing the top row first minimizes the damage Bob takes from them while fighting others.

Problem Title: Check if Two Chessboard Squares Have the Same Color
How to Draw (Opt): Write the two coordinates (e.g., a1, c3). Convert to numbers: (0,0) and (2,2). Sum them: 0 and 4. Both are even, so they match.
Draw a triangle (Heap) with exactly K slots. As you process queries, show larger values being "ejected" from the top and smaller values entering the bottom.

Problem Title: Select Cells in Grid with Maximum Score
How to Draw (Opt): List all values from the grid in descending order. For each value, try assigning it to one of the rows it appears in (if that row's bit in the mask is 0).
Draw a 2D matrix. Fill the diagonal (size 1 subarrays). Fill the next diagonal by XORing the two elements below it. Then create a second matrix to find the max in each rectangle.

Problem Title: Find Candidates for Data Scientist Position II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw candidates. Give them a checklist of required skills. If they have the skill, check the box. Filter out anyone who doesn't have 100% of the boxes checked.

Problem Title: Maximum Total Area Occupied by Pistons
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Push 4 walls (Top, Bottom, Left, Right) inward from the edges. Stop each wall the exact moment it touches a '1'. The remaining box is your answer.
Draw the string "2024-08-25". Draw scissors cutting it into three blocks. Pass each block through a "Binary Converter" box. Glue them back together.

Problem Title: Maximize Score of Numbers in Ranges
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a horizontal number line with intervals. Pick a "Score" X. Place a pin in the first interval. Measure X distance with a ruler. If the next interval starts after the ruler ends, place the next pin at the interval start. Otherwise, place it exactly where the ruler ends.

Problem Title: Reach End of Array With Maximum Score
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array as a bar chart. Stand on the first bar. Look right. Jump to the next bar that is *taller* than your current one. Repeat.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Moves to Kill All Pawns
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table where one axis is "Which pawns are dead" (as binary 1011...) and the other is "Which pawn was killed last". Fill cells by picking the best next move from the BFS distances.
Draw numbers. For '5', look at the "Increasing Chain" bucket for '4'. Attach '5' to all those chains. Add the new sum to the total. Do the same for decreasing (look at '6').

Draw a row of mountains with heights. Place your left finger on mountain i-1 and right finger on mountain i. If the left height is above threshold, circle the right index. Slide both fingers one step right.

Problem Title: Find a Safe Walk Through a Grid
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Use a pen to draw "ripples" expanding from (0,0). For each cell, write the minimum health lost to reach it. If you reach the end with a number smaller than the starting health, you win.

Draw a table where rows = length j and columns = bitsets of reachable values. Move through the array, updating the bitset by OR-ing the current value with all previous reachable values. Repeat from the end (suffix).

Problem Title: Length of the Longest Increasing Path Through Two Points
How to Draw (Opt): Plot the points. Draw a vertical and horizontal line through the target point, creating four quadrants. Only keep points in the bottom-left and top-right quadrants. Run the LIS "staircase" algorithm on each quadrant.

Problem Title: The Two Sneaky Numbers of Digitville
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two circles. In one, write 0 dots n-1. In the other, write the input. Draw lines connecting identical numbers between circles. The two "unconnected" numbers in the input are the sneaky ones.
Draw 4 slots labeled dp1, dp2, dp3, dp4. As you slide your finger over array b, update dp4 using dp3, then dp3 using dp2, and so on.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Valid Strings to Form Target I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 1D DP array. Under each index, draw "jump" arrows of various lengths based on the Trie matches found at that index. Pick the path with the fewest jumps.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Valid Strings to Form Target II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the target string. At each index, write a number L (longest match). This turns the problem into "At index i, you can jump to any j <= i+L." Use a Segment Tree to find the min jumps in that range.
Draw two tables. Draw a line from a Product row to its matching Category in the Discount table. Write the math result in a new "Final Cost" column.

Problem Title: Convert Doubly Linked List to Array II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the DLL. Put your pen on the given node. Draw a dashed line moving left until the start. From the start, draw a solid line all the way to the right, labeling each node value.
Draw a "Filter Box" representing the HashSet. Throw each word from the message into the box. If it hits a "banned" slot, increment a tally on the side. Stop when the tally hits 2.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Seconds to Make Mountain Height Zero
Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on Answer / Priority Queue
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a timeline. Pick a middle time. For each worker, draw a block showing how much "Mountain" they can eat. If the blocks together cover the mountain, try a smaller time.

Problem Title: Count Substrings That Can Be Rearranged to Contain a String I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Place two fingers (L, R). Widen R until you have the target. Every step you can shrink L while staying valid adds a "Count" for all remaining suffixes.

Problem Title: Count Substrings That Can Be Rearranged to Contain a String II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the 26-slot array. Circle the "Needs" counter. As you slide R, update a slot and, if it hits zero, decrement the counter. When the counter is zero, you've hit a "Good Window."
Draw a row of numbers. Underneath, draw a "Count Box" for each number. As you scan, update the count: "If I see a 5 and have 10 sequences ending in 4, I now have 10 new sequences ending in 5."

Problem Title: Minimum Element After Replacement With Digit Sum
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number. Draw a "Whirlpool" arrow showing digits being pulled off the end via `% 10` and added to a "Sum" bucket until the number is 0.

Problem Title: Maximize the Total Height of Unique Towers
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sorted list of limits. Place a "Cap" variable. For each limit, update `Cap = min(limit, Cap - 1)`. Tally these `Cap` values into your total sum.

Problem Title: Find the Lexicographically Smallest Valid Sequence
How to Draw (Opt): Draw `word1`. Above it, write the precomputed suffix values. As you build the result, check: "If I skip this character, can the remaining suffix still accommodate the rest of word2?"

Problem Title: Find the Occurrence of First Almost Equal Substring
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the Z-array as a series of bar charts over the string. At index i, look at the prefix bar and the suffix bar for the window. If their combined length covers the pattern (minus one mismatch), you found it.

Problem Title: Find the K-th Character in String Game I
How to Draw (Opt): Write the number K-1. Divide by 2 repeatedly to build binary. Circle every '1'. Count the circles to find the character offset.

Problem Title: Count of Substrings Containing Every Vowel and K Consonants I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Use physical brackets [ and ] for pointers. Keep a tally box of 'a,e,i,o,u' and 'consonants' that updates as brackets move.

Problem Title: Count of Substrings Containing Every Vowel and K Consonants II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Place physical brackets [ ] representing pointers. Keep a tally box for a,e,i,o,u and consonants. Shift brackets and update the tally.

Problem Title: Find the K-th Character in String Game II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a long bar for length N. Cut it in half. Mark where K sits. If it's on the right, draw an arrow mapping it to the left half. Repeat until size is 1.
Draw three small blocks. Draw funnels merging them (JOIN). Draw a bucket (GROUP BY). Draw a sorted list inside the bucket (RANK). Pick the top item.

Problem Title: Maximum Possible Number by Binary Concatenation
How to Draw (Opt): Write binary for A and B in boxes. Place A before B. Below it, place B before A. Circle the combination that forms the larger binary value.
Draw a directed graph. Take a red pen and shade the connected component from the target. Then, scan arrows. If an uncolored node points into the red blob, draw a big X.
Draw nodes as jigsaw puzzle pieces with 2, 3, or 4 pegs. Physically align the 2-peg pieces in the corners and fill inward using the connections.
Draw overlapping Venn diagrams. One circle is "Divisible by 2", another "Divisible by 4". Subtract the inner circle to find exactly GCD=2.

Problem Title: Find the Last Marked Nodes in Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a messy tree. Find the two absolute extremes and mark them A and B. Pick any random node and draw lines to A and B; one of them is the answer.

Problem Title: Construct the Minimum Bitwise Array I
How to Draw (Opt): Write the prime `p` in binary. Box the block of 1s at the far right. Cross out the leftmost '1' in that box and write a '0'. Convert back to decimal.

Problem Title: Construct the Minimum Bitwise Array II
How to Draw (Opt): Write the large prime in binary. Below it, write a binary mask that has a '1' only at the highest trailing one's position. Draw a minus sign and subtract them.

Problem Title: Find Maximum Removals From Source String
Optimised Pattern: 2D Dynamic Programming (Source Idx vs Pattern Idx)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid: Source string on the top (columns), Pattern on the left (rows). Draw diagonal arrows for matches, and horizontal arrows for skips/removals, writing the max score in the box.

Problem Title: Find the Number of Possible Ways for an Event
Optimised Pattern: Math / Combinatorics (Stirling Numbers of the Second Kind)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw N stick figures. Draw `k` empty buckets. Draw an arrow dropping figures into buckets (Stirling). Multiply by how you label the buckets (Permutation), multiply by a score multiplier box (Y).

Problem Title: Find X-Sum of All K-Long Subarrays I
Optimised Pattern: Sliding Window + Ordered Set / Hash Map
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array with a sliding bracket [ ]. Below, draw a Hash Map box. When sliding, draw a "+1" arrow for the incoming char and "-1" for outgoing. Maintain a small "Top X" VIP list next to it.

Problem Title: K-th Largest Perfect Subtree Size in Binary Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Start at the bottom leaves. Write `(True, 1)` next to them. Draw arrows pointing UP to their parents. Combine the pairs at the parent to form `(True, 2)`.

Problem Title: Count The Number of Winning Sequences
Optimised Pattern: 3D Dynamic Programming (Index, Last Move, Score Diff)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a timeline representing `i`. At a specific `i`, draw 3 buckets (Rock, Paper, Scissors). Inside each bucket, draw a number line (the score difference from -N to +N) and track the counts moving between buckets as `i` advances.

Problem Title: Find X-Sum of All K-Long Subarrays II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two boxes: VIP (max size X) and Waiting. When sliding the window, erase/update tally marks. Draw physical arrows swapping numbers between the two boxes based on the new tallies.
Draw several separate "buckets" (partitions). Write team names inside them. Physically rewrite the list inside the bucket in descending order and number them 1 to N.

Problem Title: Minimize Connected Groups by Inserting Interval
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the merged intervals as solid blocks. Create a physical paper "ruler" of length `k`. Place the start of the ruler at the end of a block. Count how many other blocks the ruler covers.

Problem Title: Find the Sequence of Strings Appeared on the Screen
How to Draw (Opt): Write the target word. In a column below, write 'a'. Next row, write the prefix + 'a', then prefix + 'b'. The shape will look like a staircase stepping down.

Problem Title: Count Substrings With K-Frequency Characters I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Place [ and ] brackets. When a letter hits count `k`, draw arrows from index 0 pointing to your left bracket. The number of arrows is the number of valid substrings you just found.

Problem Title: Minimum Division Operations to Make Array Non Decreasing
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw a Left-pointing arrow starting at the end. When a number is too big, draw a "zap" replacing it with its pre-calculated SPF. If it's still too big, draw a big red X (return -1).

Problem Title: Check if DFS Strings Are Palindromes
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the tree. Below it, draw a straight 1D array representing the flattened DFS string. Draw brackets denoting a node's boundaries. Draw a "Manacher radius" expanding from the center of those brackets.

Problem Title: Find Cities in Each State II (SQL)
Optimised Pattern: SQL GROUP BY + String Aggregation + HAVING
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a big funnel splitting data into labeled State buckets. Inside a bucket, draw a list of cities getting chained together into one long string. Keep a tally mark for prefix matches.

Problem Title: Count Substrings With K-Frequency Characters II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string with [ and ] brackets. When a letter hits `k`, draw a long bracket from index 0 all the way to `l`. That bracket represents a "batch" of answers you just found instantly without counting them 1-by-1.

Problem Title: Find the Original Typed String I
Optimised Pattern: Linear Scan / Adjacent Identical Pairs Math
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string out. Take a pen and draw a little physical "link" bridge between any two adjacent letters that are the same. Count the links. Add 1.
Draw the tree. As you walk down, drop colored breadcrumbs (one for each letter). When a node looks up, it grabs the most recent breadcrumb of its own color.
Draw a matrix (Cities x Days). To fill Day 2 for City A, look at Day 1 for City A (stay) and Day 1 for all other Cities (travel). Take the max.

Problem Title: Find the Original Typed String II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw blocks of numbers. Use a DP array to track "ways to reach length L". To update the next block, use a sliding window over the previous DP row to sum up valid combinations instantly.

Problem Title: Find the Maximum Factor Score of Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw a row above it accumulating GCD left-to-right. Draw a row below accumulating right-to-left. To "delete" index 2, just combine the box at top-index 1 and bottom-index 3.

Problem Title: Total Characters in String After Transformations I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 26 buckets. Pick up the contents, move them one bucket right. Take the 'z' bucket, dump it into 'a', and also dump a copy into 'b'.

Problem Title: Find the Number of Subsequences With Equal GCD
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 3D cube. Each horizontal slice is an element index. Each cell in the slice represents a coordinate (g1, g2). Draw arrows "falling" from the previous slice to the current one as you include or skip numbers.

Problem Title: Total Characters in String After Transformations II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 26 dots in a circle. Draw arrows showing char transitions. Then draw the 26 x 26 grid. Shade the cells that have a '1'. Highlight the squaring process (M^2, M^4, M^8).
Draw horizontal "Buckets" for each Dept. Write salaries inside. Draw a number next to each (1, 2, 2, 3) based on the rank. Circle all the '2's.

Problem Title: Find the Number of K-Even Arrays
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid: columns are 1 dots N, rows are 0 dots K. Each cell has two sub-boxes (Even/Odd). Draw arrows from (i-1, j) and (i-1, j-1) into the current cell.
Draw a balance scale. For every number you read, draw a weight on the left (even) or right (odd) side. See if the scale stays level at the end.

Problem Title: Find Minimum Time to Reach Last Room I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Write a number in each cell representing its threshold. Use a highlighter to "shade" cells as they are visited, writing the actual arrival time in a circle.

Problem Title: Find Minimum Time to Reach Last Room II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Use two different colored pens (e.g., Red for parity 0, Blue for parity 1). When you move from a Red state, the neighbor becomes a Blue state.
Draw a table where rows are digits (0-9) and columns are possible sums. For each digit, draw arrows showing how many you "give" to the even-indexed slots vs odd-indexed slots.
Draw a Venn Diagram (A, B, Shared). Pick dots from the "A-only" crescent until you hit N/2. Do the same for "B-only". If you still need dots, grab them from the "Shared" middle.

Optimised Pattern: Brute Force with Early Exit (Small Constraints)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line. At each integer, draw a small calculator box. Show the check failing until one "ticks" green.

Problem Title: Maximum Frequency of an Element After Performing Operations I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw numbers on a number line. Draw a "cloud" of size 2k around each. Slide a vertical scanner left to right to find the spot where the most clouds overlap.

Problem Title: Maximum Frequency of an Element After Performing Operations II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a huge empty number line. Only drop pins at the exact start and end of a cloud. Sweep only from pin to pin, skipping the massive empty spaces in between.
Write num. At a certain digit, change it to something higher. Calculate the "missing" factors. Draw empty slots and fill them with the smallest digits (like 9s then 8s) that cover those factors.
Draw the array. Above it, write the length of the "increasing streak" so far (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 1, 2). To check for K=2, look for two 2s that are K distance apart.
Draw the array. Color code the increasing blocks. Write the length of each block. The answer is the maximum of min(Block_i, Block_i+1) across the whole array.
Draw 3 buckets: (x-1), x, (x+1). Draw arrows showing "Sum" and "Count" flowing from neighbors into the current value bucket.

Problem Title: Count K-Reducible Numbers Less Than N
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree where each node is `(index, current_count, is_less)`. Show how the "is_less" flag prunes half the branches.
Draw a bar chart of the differences. You only pay a "cost" when a bar is taller than the bar to its left (in the same direction). Add up these positive vertical step-ups.

Problem Title: Make Array Elements Equal to Zero
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a horizontal line (the array). Put a triangle (pivot) at each 0. Write the total weight on the Left and Right sides. If they balance, it's a "Yes".
Draw a line of zeros. For a query [2, 5], write +1 at index 2 and -1 at index 6. Draw a "running total" line below that adds up these markers.

Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on Answer + Difference Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line 1 dots Q. Mark k in the middle. Below, draw a Difference Array (markers for +v and -v). Compute prefix sums. If array is zeroed, mark k "Green" and move Left.

Problem Title: Minimize the Maximum Adjacent Element Difference
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line of possible differences. Guess D=5. Look at the array blanks. "Can I pick a number that is within 5 of the left neighbor AND right neighbor?" If yes, guess a tighter D.
Draw the table. The database engine drops a net that only catches rows where the rating slot is completely empty.

Problem Title: Find Sorted Submatrices With Maximum Element at Most K
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the rows. Stick a green label on items <= K and a red label on others. Group them into buckets and only keep the buckets with all green labels.
Draw a pile of stones. Alice grabs a handful of 10. Bob grabs 9. Keep shrinking the pile by a strictly decreasing number until someone's hand hits the bottom of an empty bucket.
Draw the circle again. This time, mark each segment with a fixed weight. Use a "cut" and "subtract" method on the circle's circumference to find distances instantly.

Draw the array. Above it, draw "Water Buckets" (queries) at their start points. When a number is positive, "reach" into the bucket and pull out the longest query. Mark the decrease using a Difference Array.

Problem Title: Find the Maximum Number of Fruits Collected
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Color the diagonal. Shade the top-right triangle and bottom-left triangle separately. Write DP transitions (max of 3 neighbors) inside the cells.
Draw the array. Draw a bracket of length L. Slide it across. Then draw a bracket of length L+1. Keep a "Global Minimum" sticky note on the side.

Problem Title: Rearrange K Subsets to Form Target String
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two rows of boxes. In row 1, label each box with its string chunk. In row 2, do the same. Use lines to connect identical boxes. If all find a pair, return true.
Draw a grid for each index i. Rows = Op1 counts, Columns = Op2 counts. For a cell (k, j), draw arrows from the previous index's grid showing the best "cost-so-far".

Problem Title: Maximize Sum of Weights after Edge Removals
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a node and its children. Write the "Keep" and "Cut" scores next to each child. Draw a "Leaderboard" sorting these differences. Circle the top K or K-1 winners.
Draw a column of messy text. Draw it passing through a "Sieve" (SQL function). The output is a column of clean, capitalized text.
Draw two triangles (Heaps) meeting at the tips. Draw a "Balance Scale" between them. Next to it, draw a "Leaderboard" (SortedSet) for the mode.

Problem Title: Smallest Number With All Set Bits
How to Draw (Opt): Write N in binary. Count the digits. Draw that many empty boxes square square square. Fill them all with '1's. Calculate the decimal value.

Problem Title: Identify the Largest Outlier in an Array
How to Draw (Opt): Write the Total Sum S. Draw a box labeled "Potential Sums". For each y in the array, calculate S - 2y. Draw a line to the Frequency Map to see if that value is available.

Problem Title: Maximize the Number of Target Nodes After Connecting Trees I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw Tree 1 and Tree 2. For each node, draw rings at distance 1, 2, ... up to K. Write the "inside-ring" count next to each node. Pick the highest count from Tree 2 to add to every node in Tree 1.

Problem Title: Maximize the Number of Target Nodes After Connecting Trees II
How to Draw (Opt): Color Tree 1 like a checkerboard (Red/Blue). Do the same for Tree 2. For any node in Tree 1, the answer is (Count of same-colored nodes in T1) + (Max color count in T2).
Draw a text block. Draw a "Delimiter Mask" that highlights characters after spaces or dashes. Those highlighted bits pass through an "UPPER" filter.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make Array Values Equal to K
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a staircase where each step is a unique number from the array. Count the number of steps above the "K-line". If any value is < K, draw a big red X (return -1).

Problem Title: Minimum Time to Break Locks I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a horizontal timeline. Mark "Break Lock" events. After each event, draw an "Up Arrow" showing X increasing by K. Sum the segments.

Problem Title: Digit Operations to Make Two Integers Equal
How to Draw (Opt): Draw n as a dot. Draw a "circle" around it of numbers reachable by 1 step. Color prime dots red (dead ends). Write the "value" inside each dot.

Problem Title: Count Connected Components in LCM Graph
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the numbers. Instead of checking pairs, take '2'. Draw bridges from '2' to '4', '6', '8'. Then take '3'. Draw bridges to '6', '9'. The shared multiples merge the components automatically.
Draw the array in a circle (like a clock). For each index, draw one "teleportation" arrow that lands on the target index.

Problem Title: Maximum Area Rectangle With Point Constraints I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. Pick two points (top-left, bottom-right). Instantly check the Map for the other two corners. If present, draw the box and quickly scan the remaining points to ensure the box is empty.

Problem Title: Maximum Subarray Sum With Length Divisible by K
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. As you walk, keep a tally. Put the tally in one of k buckets based on your step number. To maximize your score, subtract the smallest tally sitting in your current modulo bucket.

Problem Title: Maximum Area Rectangle With Point Constraints II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the points. A vertical laser sweeps left to right. It records the Y-height of pairs of points. If it sees the *exact same* Y-pair later, it calculates the box area, using the tree to ensure no points sneaked in.

Problem Title: Minimum Runes to Add to Cast Spell
How to Draw (Opt): Draw nodes and arrows. Circle groups of nodes that loop into each other (SCCs). Treat each circle as one giant node. Count the circles that have absolutely zero arrows pointing *into* them.
Draw a scoreboard with two columns: Team A and Team B. As you scroll down the "Goals" table, update the counts. Highlight the rows where A > B.

Problem Title: Minimum Time to Break Locks II
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a row of locks. Flip them open one by one. Each open lock increases your "Sword Power". Try different orders of opening them, recording the total waiting time for each exact combination of open/closed locks.
Draw a horizontal line with timestamps. Draw brackets between stamps. Write the gap value inside each bracket. Circle the widest bracket.

Problem Title: Maximize Amount After Two Days of Conversions
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two separate graphs (Day 1 and Day 2). For Day 1, draw concentric circles showing the best "rate" to reach each node. Repeat for Day 2 starting from those rates.

Problem Title: Count Beautiful Splits in an Array
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Build a 2D matrix storing how many letters match starting from any two points. Put two dividers in the string. Instead of comparing letters, instantly look up the matrix cell to see if the prefix condition holds.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make Character Frequencies Equal
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 26 boxes. Below them, draw a DP array. For box i, draw an arrow from `dp[i-1]` and a "combo" arrow from `dp[i-2]` if you are moving excess characters.

Draw 3 identical grids stacked like glass panes. Layer 0 has no neutralizations, Layer 1 has one, Layer 2 has two. To fill a cell in Layer 1, look at the cell above/left in Layer 1, OR pull from Layer 0 and "spend" a neutralization to ignore a negative number.

Problem Title: Design a 3D Binary Matrix with Efficient Layer Tracking

Problem Title: Count Subarrays of Length Three With a Condition
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Place a "3-cell" physical paper template over the array. Slide it. Write "Match" or "No" under each center element.

Problem Title: Count Paths with the Given XOR Value
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the grid. For each cell, draw a small table (0 to 15). To fill cell (i, j) with XOR value V, add paths from (i-1, j) and (i, j-1) that XOR with `grid[i][j]` to reach V.

Problem Title: Check if Grid can be Cut into Sections

Problem Title: Subsequences with a Unique Middle Mode I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Lock a middle element. Look left: calculate combinations of choosing 2 numbers. Look right: do the same. Subtract invalid combinations where another number ties or beats your locked middle element's frequency.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Operations to Make Elements in Array Distinct

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Distinct Elements After Operations
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line. Sort your numbers as dots. For the first dot, move it as far left as possible (to x-k). For the next, place it at last+1 or its own x-k, whichever is higher.

Problem Title: Smallest Substring With Identical Characters I
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Group identical characters into blocks (e.g., "00000" is a block of 5). Guess a max length of 2. You need to "slice" the block of 5 every 3rd character. 5 / (2+1) = 1 slice. Sum the slices needed for all blocks.

Problem Title: Smallest Substring With Identical Characters II
Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on Answer + Edge Cases

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Matching Indices After Right Shifts
Optimised Pattern: KMP / String Matching (or O(N^2) if constraints allow)

Optimised Pattern: Recursive CTE (SQL) / Graph Cycle Detection with Visited Set.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make Columns Strictly Increasing

Problem Title: Find the Lexicographically Largest String From the Box I

Problem Title: Count the Number of Arrays with K Matching Adjacent Elements

Optimised Pattern: Row-based Parity Processing + Array Slicing. O(M · N)

Optimised Pattern: DFS with Sliding Window on Tree Paths + Hash Map. O(N)

How to Draw (Opt): Draw two arrays of numbers. Sort one ascending, sort the other descending. Draw straight vertical lines connecting index 0 to index 0, index 1 to 1. Find the max sum.

Optimised Pattern: Prefix and Suffix Sums. Precalculate even/odd sums. When you "remove" an element, the suffix even/odd sums simply swap roles!
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Write `PrefEven`, `PrefOdd` before a cut point. Write `SuffEven`, `SuffOdd` after it. To test the cut, sum `PrefEven + SuffOdd` and `PrefOdd + SuffEven`.

Problem Title: Earliest Finish Time for Land and Water Rides II (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Binary Search on the Answer. Guess a "Finish Time" T. Write a greedy O(N) checker to see if all rides can be completed within time T.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line from 0 to Max_Possible_Time. Drop a pin in the middle. If the O(N) check passes, cross out the right half. If it fails, cross out the left.

Optimised Pattern: Segment Tree with Boyer-Moore Voting or Random Sampling + Range Binary Search (using a map of indices).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a subarray. Circle 5 random elements. For each circled number, look it up in a precomputed "Sorted Indices Map" and subtract `upper_bound - lower_bound` to get the count.

Optimised Pattern: Two-pointer approach or Hash Map tracking based on the property target - (arr[i] + arr[j]) .
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Point to index i . For the rest of the array, place pointers L and R at the ends and move them inward based on the sum.

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming with a Fenwick Tree or Segment Tree to find the maximum shipments ending at index `i`.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Below it, draw a Fenwick Tree (series of boxes). For each element, look up the "best previous score" from the boxes, add 1, and update the boxes.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy algorithm with a Queue or Monotonic Stack, focusing on the nearest available character that satisfies the activation rule.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. For the current active index, draw arrows to the nearest 'target' characters. Pick the shortest arrow, move there, and repeat.

Optimised Pattern: Segment Tree or Square Root Decomposition where each "node" or "bucket" stores partial Trionic counts.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Group it into 4 large boxes. If an element in Box 1 changes, only redraw Box 1. To get the total, just sum the 4 boxes.

Optimised Pattern: Sliding Window with a single counter for "duplicate pairs".
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Use two fingers (L and R). Move R right. When you see two identical numbers together, write "Doubles: 1". Move R again. If you see another double, move L until one double is "pushed out" of the window.

Optimised Pattern: Hash Map Aggregation / SQL Group By with HAVING clause. Compute statistics for all books in one pass.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a box for each Book. Read through the review list once. Drop a tally mark in the corresponding Book box for either "1-star" or "5-star". Calculate final ratios from the tallies.

Optimised Pattern: In-place row swapping within the specified submatrix boundaries.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 4x4 square inside a larger grid. Draw a horizontal "mirror line" in the center of that square. Draw arrows showing row 1 and row 4 of that square swapping places.

Problem Title: Maximum K to Sort a Permutation (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) of the displacements. Find the distance each element needs to move and find the GCD of all those distances.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Point to '5' at index 2. Its home is index 8. Distance = 6. Point to '3' at index 0. Its home is index 9. Distance = 9. Find GCD(6, 9) = 3. Final K = 3.

Problem Title: Maximum Total from Optimal Activation Order (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Greedy Algorithm with a Max-Priority Queue. At any step, activate the item that provides the highest immediate gain/value based on current state.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a list of items with values. Put them in a "Priority Queue" (a funnel that always puts the largest on top). Pull the top item, update world state, repeat.

Optimised Pattern: Mirroring + Middle-Digit Increment. Construct the palindrome from the first half of N, then adjust based on "Special" digit constraints.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number (e.g., 12345). Draw a vertical dashed line in the middle. "Fold" the left side (12) onto the right side to get 12321. Adjust the '3' if needed.

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming (Knapsack variant) or Meet-in-the-Middle if N is around 30-40.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two lists of possible sums. Sort both. Use a finger on each list to find pairs that sum as close to the "Weight Limit" as possible without exceeding it.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy with Set Cover or Bitmask DP (if dimensions are small). Focus on covering the "bottleneck" (uncovered cells with the fewest neighbors).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid. Shade cells that need coverage. Pick a sensor location, draw its "light" area. Subtract those from the "dark" total. Repeat until no shaded cells remain.

Optimised Pattern: Algebraic Simplification + Binary Search / Fenwick Tree. Re-write the property as a range: arr[j] must be in [arr[i]/2, 2*arr[i]] .
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a sorted number line. Mark x . Draw a bracket from x/2 to 2x . Count the dots inside the bracket using bisect_right - bisect_left .

Problem Title: Minimum Cost Path with Edge Reversals (Med)
Optimised Pattern: 0-1 BFS or Dijkstra's Algorithm on a modified graph where every directed edge (u -> v) with weight 0 has a "ghost" edge (v -> u) with weight 1.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a graph with solid arrows (cost 0). Draw dotted arrows in the reverse direction (cost 1). Use a Deque: push 0-cost edges to the front, 1-cost to the back.

Optimised Pattern: Dijkstra + Auxiliary "Teleport Hub" nodes. Instead of V x V edges, connect all nodes to one "Hub" with specific entry/exit costs.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 5 nodes in a circle. Draw one big node in the center. Draw arrows from outer nodes to the center (Cost X) and from center to outer (Cost Y). Run Dijkstra.

Problem Title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock using Strategy (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming / State Machine. State = `(Day, HasStock)`. Use a simple greedy one-pass if unlimited trades are allowed.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line graph. Whenever the line goes up, draw a vertical green arrow representing profit. Total profit = sum of all green arrows.

Problem Title: XOR After Range Multiplication Queries I (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Segment Tree or Fenwick Tree with Lazy Propagation. Store XOR values in nodes and apply multiplications as "Lazy" updates.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a binary tree over an array. Circle the nodes that cover range [L, R]. Write "x K" next to them. If a node is XOR of its children, update it in O(1).

Problem Title: Minimum Sum After Divisible Sum Deletions (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming (Remainder DP). State = `dp[i][remainder]`. Find the maximum sum we can delete that leaves a remainder of R.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with columns 0, 1, ..., K-1. As you process numbers, update the "Max Sum" for each column. Final answer = Total Sum - `dp[N][0]`.

Problem Title: XOR After Range Multiplication Queries II (Hard)
Optimised Pattern: Segment Tree where each node stores a "Bit Count" array (size 32 or 64). Multiplication by a power of 2 becomes a "Bit Shift" on the array.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 32 Segment Trees stacked vertically. When a range is multiplied by 2, show bit 0 moving to bit 1, bit 1 to bit 2, etc. across the whole range.

Problem Title: Determine if a Simple Graph Exists (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Havel-Hakimi Algorithm. Sort the degree sequence, then repeatedly "consume" the highest degree by connecting it to the next highest degrees.
How to Draw (Opt): List degrees: [3, 3, 2, 2]. Remove 3, subtract 1 from next 3: becomes [2, 1, 1]. Repeat until the list is all zeros (Success) or impossible (Fail).

Optimised Pattern: Hash Set Aggregation. For each customer, collect `transaction_month` into a Set. Filter customers where `Set.size == 12`.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a customer ID. Next to it, draw 12 tiny boxes. As you read the logs, fill the boxes. If all are full, circle the ID.

Problem Title: GCD of Odd and Even Sums (Easy)
Optimised Pattern: Euclidean Algorithm for GCD. `gcd(a, b)` where you repeatedly take the remainder until one reaches zero.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two bars (SumOdd, SumEven). Use the Euclidean subtraction: `while b: a, b = b, a % b`. Write the steps: `30 % 20 = 10`, `20 % 10 = 0`. GCD is 10.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy Frequency Distribution. Sort elements by frequency. Place the most frequent elements into different groups first.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw K boxes. Count frequencies: `3: [7, 7, 7], 2: [5, 5]`. Put one '7' in Box 1, one in Box 2, one in Box 3. Put '5' in Box 1 and Box 2.

Optimised Pattern: Monotonic Stack to find the next/previous greater/smaller elements in O(N), then Dijkstra/DP on those specific edges.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array of heights. Use a stack to find the "Next Greater" index for each. Draw only ONE jump arrow from i to its Next Greater. Repeat for Next Smaller.

Optimised Pattern: Maximum Flow / Bipartite Matching. Model robots as one set of nodes, walls as another, and find the maximum matching/coverage.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a "Source" node. Connect it to Robots. Connect Robots to the Walls they can destroy. Connect Walls to a "Sink". The max flow value is your answer.

Optimised Pattern: Single-pass Hash Map / Frequency Array. Count all frequencies first, then perform a single pass to filter.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 26 small boxes. As you read the string, put a tally mark in the box. Then, read the string again and only write characters whose box has a count > K.

Optimised Pattern: Modulo-based Frequency Array. Use `n % 10` to strip digits and an array of size 10 to store counts.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 10 slots. Take `1223`. `3` goes to Slot 3. `2` to Slot 2. `2` to Slot 2. `1` to Slot 1. Slot 1 and 3 are tied for least frequent.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy Sorting / Hash Map counting. Pair cards based on their first and second letters to maximize your "counter-plays".
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 26 nodes (letters). Draw an arrow for each card (e.g., 'AB' is an arrow from A to B). The game is about finding paths or cycles in this letter graph.

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming / BFS with State. State = `(row, col, direction)`. Since the path is "twisted", we track our entering vector.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a grid. In each cell, draw 4 "ports" (N, S, E, W). Use a arrow to show how entering North reflects out East due to a '/' mirror. Mark cells as "visited" for that specific direction.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Equalize Binary String (Hard)
Optimised Pattern: Greedy "Flip-and-Carry" or Difference Array. Start from the left; if the current bit doesn't match the target, flip the remainder of the string.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the string. Sweep left to right. When you hit a mismatch, increment a "Flip Counter" and move on. The counter tells you the "current" reality of the remaining bits.

Optimised Pattern: Language-native sort with a Custom Key/Comparator.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 0 in the center. Place -5 and 3. Since 3 is closer to 0 than -5 is, it comes first in the sorted absolute list. Order: [3, -5].

Optimised Pattern: Hash Map (ID to Rank) or Tuple Sorting. Store (Time, ID) pairs and sort them directly.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table with two columns: Time and ID . Sort by the Time column. Read the ID column top to bottom.

Optimised Pattern: Square Root Factorization + Dynamic Programming. Only check up to √N to find all factors, then use DP to find balanced subsets.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line. Put √N in the middle. Find factors on the left. For each, draw an arc to its "partner" on the right. This finds all factors in O(√N).

Problem Title: Maximum Product of Two Integers With No Common Bits (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Hash Map of Bitmasks. For each unique bitmask, store the maximum integer found. Then compare all pairs of bitmasks.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 32 bins. Drop numbers into bins based on their binary pattern. Compare Bin A and Bin B. If (BinA_mask & BinB_mask) == 0 , multiply their max values.

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming with Counting (Combinatorics). Group elements by their remainder when divided by the "beauty" difference K, then solve as a "House Robber" variant.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw circles for numbers. Draw lines between numbers that "conflict" (e.g., distance K). This creates several separate paths. Calculate valid subsets for each path using DP and multiply results.

Problem Title: Sum of Weighted Modes in Subarrays (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Frequency Tracking with Contribution Counting. For each element, mathematically calculate how many subarrays it serves as the mode.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Highlight all "5"s. Draw boundaries showing the largest area where "5" remains the most frequent (or tied). Use precomputed index lists for each number.

Optimised Pattern: Disjoint Set Union (DSU) with Path Compression. For every session connection, union the two nodes and decrement the total cluster count.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two 1D arrays: `parent` and `size`. When A and B connect, point A's root to B's root. Show the "Component Count" variable dropping by 1.

Optimised Pattern: Median-Based Greedy approach. The total cost is minimized when all elements are changed to the median of the array.
How to Draw (Opt): Sort the array. Pick the middle element. Calculate `abs(arr[i] - median)` for every element and sum them up.

Optimised Pattern: Two-Pointer Greedy Scan or Edit Distance variant. If only specific operations are allowed (like moving to front), focus on the "Longest Matching Suffix".
How to Draw (Opt): Draw String A and String B. Place pointer `i` at the end of A and `j` at the end of B. If `A[i] == B[j]`, move both left. If not, only move `i` left. The remaining `j` count is the number of moves.

Optimised Pattern: Precompute "Longest Decreasing" from left and "Longest Increasing" from right for every index.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. For each index, write two numbers: `L` (how many smaller items to the left) and `R` (how many smaller items to the right). Total bowls = sum of `L * R` for all i .

Optimised Pattern: Combinatorial Construction. Generate palindromes directly by mirroring the "first half" of the binary bits.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw bit slots: [1][ ][ ][ ][1] . Note that the middle bits can be any combination. Use a loop to count combinations for each possible bit-length up to N.

Problem Title: Smallest Absent Positive Greater Than Average (Easy)
Optimised Pattern: Calculate average, throw array into a Hash Set, and start checking from ceil(avg) + 1 .
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line. Mark the 'Average'. Put all array numbers into a Hash Set. Start at the 'Average', take one step at a time. The first number not in the Set is the answer.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy with Frequency Maps or Dynamic Programming. Count the excess of each item type and discard only the necessary surplus.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a frequency table. Write "Target Count = K". For each item, if `count > K`, subtract `count - K` from inventory. If `count < K`, subtract all. Sum the subtractions.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy Strategy: Earliest Deadline First (EDF) or Shortest Processing Time (SPT).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a timeline. Sort tasks by deadline. Place them on the timeline one by one. If any task's "End Time" exceeds its "Deadline", that schedule is invalid.

Optimised Pattern: Linear Basis (Gaussian Elimination for XOR). Find a set of basis vectors that can represent any possible XOR sum of the array.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 31 slots. For each number, find its highest set bit B. If slot B is empty, put it there. If not, XOR it with slot B and repeat with the new number. Finally, greedily XOR from the top slot to 0 to get the max.

Problem Title: Minimum Index Sum of Common Elements (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Hash Map for List A (Value -> Index). Scan List B and look up values in the Map in O(1).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a Map: {"Pizza": 0, "Burger": 1} . Read List B. When you see "Burger", get 1 from the map. Sum it with current index in B. Keep track of the minimum sum found.

Problem Title: Earliest Time to Finish One Task (Easy)
Optimised Pattern: Precompute dependencies and apply a Greedy "Earliest Start" calculation.
How to Draw (Opt): Calculate StartTime + Duration for every task. Store them in an array and use a simple min() function.

Problem Title: Maximize Sum of At Most K Distinct Elements (Easy)
Optimised Pattern: Greedy Sorting. Sort the array descending and pick the largest values until you hit the K distinct limit.
How to Draw (Opt): Sort the whole array largest to smallest. Walk down the list. Add to sum and keep a count of how many unique numbers you've seen. Stop when unique == K .

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming (Knapsack variant) or Combinatorial Math. Since capping is a uniform rule, use the frequency of elements to calculate contributions.
How to Draw (Opt): Identify items > Cap. If there are M such items, any subsequence involving any of them contributes the Cap value. Use DP to find sums of the small items and combine with the 2^M combinations of the capped ones.

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming with Frequency Maps or Combinatorics. Calculate combinations of each element frequency F independently and sum them.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw columns for each unique number. For a target frequency F, count how many ways to pick F items from each column using nCr. Multiply the (ways + 1 for excluding) across all numbers and subtract 1 (empty set).

Optimised Pattern: Datetime library or Formula-based difference. `(Date2 - Date1).days * rate`.
How to Draw (Opt): Convert both dates to "Days since Year 0". Day2 - Day1 = TotalDays. Multiplied by rate_map[item_type] .

Problem Title: Bitwise OR of Even Numbers in an Array (Easy)
Optimised Pattern: Single-pass iteration with a running bitwise OR variable and a parity check.
How to Draw (Opt): Initialize res = 0 . Draw an arrow through the array. At each step, if num % 2 == 0 , res |= num .

Optimised Pattern: Kadane's Algorithm or Sliding Window with Prefix Sums. If Value = Sum, it's strictly Kadane's.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Keep current_sum and max_sum . If current_sum < 0 , reset it to 0. Always update max_sum with the current peak.

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming (Interval DP). `dp[i][j]` stores whether the subarray from i to j can be transformed into the target.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a 2D grid. The cell (i, j) represents the range [i, j]. Build the grid diagonally, starting from length 1 up to N, checking if sub-ranges can merge.

Optimised Pattern: Divide and Conquer or Segment Tree with "Monotonic Property" optimization. Focus on boundaries where the XOR value changes (at most 32 times).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. For index i, draw arrows pointing to the next indices where the XOR sum *actually* changes. Skip the redundant indices in between.

Optimised Pattern: Single-pass Frequency Array (size 26) + Max Frequency tracking.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw 26 small squares. As you scan, tally them. Keep max_freq updated. At the end, iterate the 26 squares and print those equal to max_freq .

Optimised Pattern: Iterative Dynamic Programming (Bottom-Up). `dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2] + dp[i-3]`.
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a row of boxes. Fill boxes 1, 2, 3 with base cases. Box 4 is the sum of 1+2+3. Box 5 is the sum of 2+3+4. Keep sliding.

Problem Title: Distinct Points Reachable After Substring Removal (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sums of Coordinates. Calculate (x, y) for all prefixes and suffixes. Endpoint = Prefix[i-1] + (Total - Prefix[j]) .
How to Draw (Opt): Precompute pref[i] as the (x, y) after i steps. For a removal of [i, j], the new endpoint is pref[i-1] + (pref[N] - pref[j]) . Put these in a Set.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy Analysis of Parity. An alternating sum is (EvenSum - OddSum) . Swapping only helps if you move a large number from an "Odd" (negative) position to an "Even" (positive) position.
How to Draw (Opt): Find min(arr[even_indices]) and max(arr[odd_indices]) . If max_odd > min_even , swapping them increases the total sum by 2 * (max_odd - min_even) .

Problem Title: Maximum Distance Between Unequal Words I (Easy)
Optimised Pattern: Single-pass: Find the first and last occurrence of different words. The max distance is either LastIndex - FirstIndex or LastIndex - SecondFirstIndex .
How to Draw (Opt): Draw an array. Point to arr[0] and arr[N-1] . If they differ, distance = N-1. If not, scan inward from both ends to find the first mismatch.

Optimised Pattern: Horner's Method for Polynomial Evaluation. Process the string character by character, multiplying the current result by the base and adding the new digit.
How to Draw (Opt): Start with res = 0 . For each char c : res = res * base + digit(c) . This handles any string length linearly.

Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sums. Calculate all cumulative sums once. Sum of right half = TotalSum - PrefixSum[i] .
How to Draw (Opt): Precompute total_sum . Draw an arrow through the array. At index i, left_sum += arr[i] and right_sum = total_sum - left_sum . Find the i where abs(left - right) is smallest.

Optimised Pattern: Dynamic Programming (DP). `dp[i][direction]` = number of zigzags ending at index i going "up" or "down".
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a row of boxes. Each box has two slots: `Up` and `Down`. For arr[i] , look at all arr[j] < arr[i] and add their `Down` counts to your `Up` slot.

Optimised Pattern: DP + Segment Tree or Fenwick Tree. Use a coordinate-compressed Segment Tree to query the sum of `Up/Down` counts for all values smaller/larger than the current element in O(log N).
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a Segment Tree where the leaf nodes represent the sorted values of the array. As you process each arr[i] , query the tree, update your current count, and then "insert" your new result back into the tree.
Draw an array. Underneath, draw one box named "SUM". Cross out and update SUM as you move left to right.

Problem Title: Longest Subsequence With Non-Zero Bitwise XOR (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw whole array inside a bracket. Calculate total. If 0, cross out the first non-zero number and re-bracket.
Draw a tall open box (Stack). Write letters from bottom up. When top elements form a match, erase/cross them out.

Problem Title: Count No-Zero Pairs That Sum to N (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line 1 to N. Draw arched lines connecting 1 & N-1, 2 & N-2. Cross out arcs with '0'.
Draw a horizontal timeline. Draw a bracket `[ ]` that expands to the right and drags its left tail when > 60.

Problem Title: Maximum Distance Between Unequal Words in Array II (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Two Pointers / Hash Map (First & Last Occurrence)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw a large bracket from first to last element. Shrink the bracket bounds if the endpoints are identical.
Draw string. Draw a rectangle over valid chars. Tally score inside. Drag right edge, then drag left edge to keep score equal.
Draw a 2D table where rows/cols are indices. Fill cells with sequence lengths, tracing back via map lookups.
Draw a tree node. Left child is smaller score, right is larger. Keep counts in nodes. Trace path down for queries.
Draw an empty array DP. For each cell, look back at previous cells, apply factor math, and write the max value.

Optimised Pattern: Greedy + Priority Queue (Min-Heap of negative values)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a running total. Draw a bucket (Heap) for negative numbers. When total < 0, cross out the biggest negative in the bucket and add it back to total.

Problem Title: Sum of Elements With Frequency Divisible by K (Easy)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two columns: "Number" and "Tally Marks". Scan array, add tallies. Then circle rows where tallies divide by K.
Draw string. Below it, write running sum (+1/-1). Circle duplicate sum numbers. The distance between them is the substring length.

Optimised Pattern: Bitmasking + Hash Map / Advanced State Tracking
How to Draw (Opt): Write string. Keep a tally of even/odd (E/O). E.g., E,O -> O,O. Store the E/O state. Find matching past states.
Draw a tree. As you draw arrows DOWN to children, carry a "backpack" number (the sum). Update the backpack if current node is a square.

Optimised Pattern: Hash Map Aggregation / Sorting by User
How to Draw (Opt): Draw large squares (buckets) labeled with Customer IDs. Drop timestamps in, sort them, and draw small arcs between adjacent numbers to check differences.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make the Array Beautiful (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw array. Move a finger (pointer) left to right. When pairs match on a virtual even index, tally a mark on a "Deletes" scoreboard and skip ahead.
Draw a big circle (Set) with numbers scattered inside. Write a list `K, 2K, 3K`. Put a checkmark next to each if it's in the circle. Stop at the first X.
Draw sequence. Below it, plot a line graph going up (+1) and down (-1). Draw a horizontal line connecting two peaks at the exact same altitude.

Problem Title: Lexicographically Smallest Permutation Greater Than Target (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw array. Draw an arrow leftward from the end until the number drops (Pivot). Find smallest number bigger than Pivot on right. Swap. Bracket the right side and write "FLIP".
Write the array. Below it, write the running "state code". When you write a code you've written before, draw a wide arc connecting them.
Draw string. Move finger left to right. Stop at the first "suboptimal" character. Bracket it to the next target point, draw a swap arrow.

Problem Title: Maximize Sum of Squares of Digits (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw empty slots for digits. Fill them with '9's from left to right until your digit limit/sum limit is completely exhausted.
Write Target on the right, Start on the left. Draw arrows pointing *leftward*, dividing or subtracting optimally until you hit start values.

Problem Title: Count Ways to Choose Coprime Integers from Rows (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Write available primes. Next to rows, write prime-masks for numbers. Draw a DP table mapping [RowIndex][PrimeMask]. Fill counts.
Draw a number. Divide by 10 to drop the last digit into a bucket. If bucket has a non-zero, push it onto a new reconstructed pile.
Draw two horizontal tracks (Top: Add, Bottom: Sub). Write numbers along the top. Draw zig-zag arrows between tracks updating the max possible sum at each step.

Problem Title: Stable Subarrays With Equal Boundary and Interior Sum (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw array and Prefix Array below it. Draw a Hash Map bucket. Calculate the "target" math for L and drop it in the bucket. As R moves, check the bucket for matches.

Problem Title: Count Distinct Subarrays Divisible by K in Sorted Array (Hard)
Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sum Modulo K + Trie/String Hashing
How to Draw (Opt): Draw array. Below it, write running sum % K. Connect identical modulo numbers with an arc. Feed the elements under the arc into a Set to check for uniqueness.
Draw an empty array. Fill left to right. To fill a box, draw arrows looking back at allowed jump ranges. Pick the highest number, add current box's calories, write it down.

Optimised Pattern: Hash Set or Boolean Array Check
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a big circle (Hash Set). Write array numbers inside it. Write a list 1 to N. Put a checkmark if the number is in the circle, X if it's missing.

Problem Title: Maximum Product of Three Elements After One Replacement (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw sorted array. Put a bracket over the 3 rightmost elements. Put a bracket over the 2 leftmost and 1 rightmost. Compare their multiplied totals.

Problem Title: Minimum Time to Complete All Deliveries (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number line of possible times. Pick the middle value. Do a quick test. Cross out the half of the line that is no longer possible. Repeat until narrowed to one point.

Problem Title: Lexicographically Smallest Palindromic Permutation Greater Than Target (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Cut it in half. Ignore the right side. Run the "swap and reverse" permutation trick on the left half. Then copy the left half, flip it backward, and write it as the right half.

Problem Title: Lexicographically Smallest String After Reverse II (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Move your finger left to right along the string. Stop at a "bad" letter. Scan ahead for the best replacement. Draw a sweeping arc connecting them and physically rewrite the reversed chunk.

Problem Title: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements III (Easy)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw sorted array. Circle the exact middle number (the median). Draw arrows from all other numbers pointing inward to the circled median. Tally the distances.

Problem Title: Count Subarrays With Majority Element I (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Pick a target number. Write +1 under it, -1 under others. Plot a running sum on a line graph. Valid subarrays are paths between a lower dot and a higher dot.

Problem Title: Longest Non-Decreasing Subarray After Replacing at Most One Element (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Above it, write streak counts moving left-to-right `[1,2,3...]`. Below it, write streak counts moving right-to-left `[...,3,2,1]`. Pick a number to "replace" and bridge the top and bottom streaks surrounding it.

Problem Title: Count Subarrays With Majority Element II (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw array and Prefix sums. Next to it, draw a Fenwick Tree as an array of bins. To check a prefix sum, query the bins for values strictly smaller than it, add the count to total, then drop the current prefix into its respective bin.

Problem Title: Minimum Distance Between Three Equal Elements I (Easy)
Optimised Pattern: Hash Map with Index Lists / Sliding Window of Size 3
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw buckets for each unique number. Drop indices into their specific buckets. When a bucket gets 3 numbers, subtract the first from the last. Keep the smallest result.

Problem Title: Find Kth Character in Expanded String (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Reverse Recursive Math / Divide and Conquer
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a long block. Cut in half. If K is on the right, draw an arrow to the exact mirrored spot on the left and write "+1 shift". Repeat halving until K is at index 0.

Problem Title: Minimum String Length After Balanced Removals (Med)

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Flips to Reverse Binary String (Easy)

Problem Title: Total Waviness of Numbers in Range I (Med)

Problem Title: Lexicographically Smallest Negated Permutation that Sums to Target (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): List the numbers. At each number, calculate: "If I pick '-', can the rest of the numbers still reach the Target?" If yes, pick '-' and move right. If no, pick '+'.

Problem Title: Total Waviness of Numbers in Range II (Hard)
Optimised Pattern: Advanced Digit DP with Modulo/State constraints

Problem Title: Concatenate Non-Zero Digits and Multiply by Sum I (Easy)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a number box. As digits drop out of the bottom, if digit != 0, put it in the "New Number" box. Otherwise, just add it to the "Running Sum" bucket.

Problem Title: Find Maximum Balanced XOR Subarray Length (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw array. Underneath, write running XOR (e.g., 5, 2, 7, 2...). Circle the two "2"s. Draw a bracket between them. That's a XOR-0 subarray.

Problem Title: Concatenate Non-Zero Digits and Multiply by Sum II (Med)

Problem Title: Count Elements With at Least K Greater Values (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw sorted array. Write indices 0 to N-1 above. For any number, the index tells you how many are "to its left" (greater).

Problem Title: Minimum Absolute Distance Between Mirror Pairs (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw arrows connecting i and N-1-i. Sort the entire array. Take the two middle-most elements and place them in the first mirror pair, then the next two, and so on.
Draw dp array. For dp[i] , look back at dp[j] . Draw a "median-finding" box for the range [j, i] and add that cost to dp[j] .

Problem Title: Maximum Total Sum With Threshold Constraints
How to Draw (Opt): Draw numbers sorted largest to smallest. Draw "buckets" representing thresholds. Drop the largest numbers into the buckets until they hit the "cap line."

Optimised Pattern: Hash Map with Tuple Keys / Bitsets

Draw a Sieve grid (1-100). Cross out non-primes. For a target number, check the grid, then check each digit against the "Prime Digit" list.

Problem Title: Minimum Operations to Make Binary Palindrome (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the binary string. Draw arcs connecting mirror indices. If the bits at the end of an arc are different, put a tally mark in the "Flip" box.

Problem Title: Maximize Points After Choosing K Tasks (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a "Pool" of tasks. Draw a "Bucket" for your K choices. Pick the largest task from the pool, drop it in the bucket, and repeat K times.

Problem Title: Minimum Inversion Count in Subarrays of Fixed Length (Hard)
Optimised Pattern: Sliding Window + Fenwick Tree / Segment Tree
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a window moving across the array. Below it, draw a Fenwick Tree. When a number enters, "Ask" the tree how many numbers are larger. When it leaves, "Tell" the tree to remove it.
Draw two numbers. Show their bits swapping positions (Bit 0 with Bit N, etc.). Compare the results directly.

Problem Title: Largest Prime from Consecutive Prime Sum (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Sieve of Eratosthenes + Sliding Window / Prefix Sums
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a horizontal line of prime numbers. Below it, draw a Prefix Sum line. Draw an elastic bracket over the prime line; if the sum it covers is prime, mark it as a candidate.
Draw a grid. In each cell, write two numbers: "Ways to reach" and "Total Score so far". Use the top and left neighbors to update both numbers.

Problem Title: Maximum Subgraph Score in a Tree (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Start at the leaves. If a leaf is positive, "send" its value up to its parent. If negative, send 0. The parent adds its own value to the incoming sums.

Problem Title: Maximum Number of Equal Length Runs (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Bracket identical neighbors (e.g., "Two 1s", "Three 2s"). Write the lengths in a list. Tally how many times each length appears in a frequency table.

Problem Title: Absolute Difference Between Maximum and Minimum K Elements (Easy)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array and sort it. Draw a fixed-width bracket covering K elements. Slide it from left to right, measuring the distance between the two ends of the bracket.

Problem Title: Reverse Words With Same Vowel Count (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Grouping by Vowel Count + Reverse Pointers
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the sentence. Above each word, write its vowel count. Circle all words with "2" vowels. Draw arrows swapping the first circled word with the last, second with second-to-last, etc.

Problem Title: Minimum Moves to Balance Circular Array (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Prefix Sums on Doubled Array + Median of Differences
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array twice side-by-side. Calculate prefix sums of (arr[i] - average) . Find the median value of these prefix sums. The cost is the sum of absolute distances to this median.

Problem Title: Minimum Deletions to Make Alternating Substring (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Write the string. Below it, write the pattern "0101...". Draw a line connecting the first '0' in the string to the '0' in the pattern, then the next '1', and so on. Any skipped characters are deletions.

Problem Title: Minimum Distance Excluding One Maximum Weighted Edge (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two versions of the graph: "Layer 0" (haven't used the skip) and "Layer 1" (have used the skip). Draw directed edges from Layer 0 to Layer 1 representing the "skipped" edge.

Problem Title: Minimum Number of Operations to Have Distinct Elements (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Start at the end. Move left, adding numbers to a circle. Stop when you try to add a number that is already in the circle. Everything to the left of your finger must be removed.

Problem Title: Maximum Sum of Three Numbers Divisible by Three (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw three boxes labeled "Rem 0", "Rem 1", "Rem 2". Sort the numbers into them. Pick the top 3 from Rem 0; compare with picking the top 1 from each; compare with top 3 from Rem 1, etc.
Write the string. Count the '1's. Draw a new string of the same length, filling the first N slots with '1's and the rest with '0's. This is your "perfect" swap result.

Problem Title: Last Remaining Integer After Alternating Deletion Operations (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a line representing 1..N. Write "Head = 1, Step = 1". Show the "Head" jumping as numbers are deleted. Every round, the "Head" moves by the current "Step" size under specific conditions.
Draw a "Number" box and a "Reverse" box. Use a loop to pop digits from the bottom of "Number" and push them into the top of "Reverse".

Problem Title: Minimum Deletion Cost to Make All Characters Equal (Med)
Optimised Pattern: Total Cost - Max Character Frequency Cost
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a bar chart where the X-axis is 'a-z' and the Y-axis is the total cost for that letter. Find the tallest bar. The "Minimum Deletion" is the sum of all other bars.

Problem Title: Minimum Swaps to Avoid Forbidden Values (Hard)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw two columns of dots. Draw lines between them. Put an 'X' on forbidden lines. Give each line a cost (0 or 1). Find the set of lines that connects every dot with the lowest total cost.

Problem Title: Total Sum of Interaction Cost in Tree Groups (Hard)
Optimised Pattern: Tree DP + Centroid Decomposition / Contribution of Edges
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. On each edge, write a number: (Nodes on the left) x (Nodes on the right). Multiply that by the edge weight. Sum all the results on the edges.

Problem Title: Find Diameter Endpoints of a Tree (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a tree. Start at the root. Draw an arrow to the leaf furthest away (Node A). Now, starting from Node A, draw a long arrow to the leaf furthest from it (Node B).

Draw the array. Above it, write the "running score" from left to right. Below it, write the "running score" from right to left. Find the column where the top and bottom numbers add up to the highest total.

Problem Title: Minimum Cost to Acquire Required Items (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw a table where the header is bitmasks (001, 010, 011...). For each item, draw arrows showing how it "flips" the bits and updates the cost in the table.
Draw a "Remainder" bucket. Start with 1. Multiply by 10, add 1, then take the remainder of N and put it back in the bucket. Count how many times you do this until the bucket is 0.

Problem Title: Number of Balanced Integers in a Range (Hard)

Problem Title: Find Users with High Token Usage (Easy)

Problem Title: Minimum Subarray Length With Distinct Sum At Least K (Med)
How to Draw (Opt): Draw the array. Draw a bracket [ ] . Move the right side until you have K distinct numbers. Now drag the left side forward until you're about to lose a distinct number. The size of the bracket is your current "Best".

Problem Title: Count Routes to Climb a Rectangular Grid (Hard)

Problem Title: Sum of K-Digit Numbers in a Range (Hard)
